title: Customer Experience & Operations Associate (E-commerce Coffee Company) *Full Time, Remote | Long-Term Growth Opportunity
description:
Are you ready…

To start a role that motivates and excites you?

And a position where we invest in your learning, education, and growth?



Then keep reading, we want to work with you!





WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS POSITION:

This is not your typical support role — you’ll work directly with our founders, influence company-wide systems, and gain exposure to every part of the business.



Incredible company culture:

• Small, close team with collaborative, friendly and open culture.

• We believe the biggest asset of a company is our people. We focus on helping each tea
mber reach their full potential by investing in their learning, growth, skills, and education. When we grow as individuals, we succeed as a team.

• Not a fan of office politics or drama? We’re on the same page. Instead of wasting time with that, we focus on solving problems and reaching goals

• 100% remote, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of working from home



Pay, benefits, progression, and long-term growth:

• Competitive salary

• Benefits including up to 16 days a year Paid Time off

• Progression as we grow: greater responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and increased pay.

• Feel empowered, make decisions, have an impact

• Grow with the company and live up to your potential!

• Join an exciting, fast-growing company!







ABOUT US:

We’re a young, ambitious, and small (but growing!) company that’s making a massive impact in ecommerce. 



Since 2015 we’ve been obsessed with building award-winning products and awesome brands that people fall in love with. We have 2 primary brands in our portfolio: 

(1) an international award-winning coffee products brand 
 / 
).

(2) an educational coaching program that helps entrepreneurs grow their businesses to create a positive impact in the world!



Our two recent flagship products became the world’s most successful launches in their categories. We’ve shipped more than 200,000 orders worldwide and we’re continuing to expand.



Our culture is transparent and candid. You’re always welcome (and encouraged!) to ask questions or express a difference of opinion. Learning and growth is a cornerstone of our culture, both personally and professionally. We work hard, and we have fun!







WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented team player to fill this role—someone who thrives on solving problems, staying organized, and making things run smoothly across departments.



Do you love helping customers, supporting a team, and keeping operations organized? Do you get satisfaction from making systems work better and “getting things done”? If yes, we want you on our team!



You’re probably the type of person who:

• Enjoys supporting others and finding solutions to problems

• Has strong communication and organizational skills

• Likes working behind the scenes to make sure everything runs like clockwork

• Is excited to wear multiple hats and be part of a growing, fast-moving company

• Has worked directly with founders or in a startup, and enjoys that fast-paced, flexible vibe

We’re not just looking for someone who can support our team, we’re looking for someone to be proactive with taking ownership of tasks, solve problems independently, and grow into larger responsibilities over time.







YOUR OPPORTUNITY AND ROLE ON THE TEAM:

Our culture is collaborative, friendly, and open. We hire people who are smart, ambitious, and have a strong desire to learn and grow.



Sound like you? This is an exciting long-term opportunity!



This is a fully remote position. You’ll work directly with our founders and collaborate with our small but mighty team to support customer happiness, streamline operations, and take on projects that help the business grow. 



This is a foundational role — one where you’ll gain visibility into all parts of the company.



As you grow, your role will grow with you, based on your interests and strengths:

• Want to work improving our systems and processes? Awesome!

• Have ideas on how to improve our website? Fantastic!

• Want to learn how to manage projects? Let’s go!



We’re not just hiring for a role — we’re hiring for potential. 



Your responsibilities will span 4 key areas: customer experience (30-40%), operations/logistics (20-30%), executive support (10-20%), and special projects (10-20%) — giving you exposure across the business. These percentages are estimates and you can expect your day-to-day to shift and evolve as the business grows and as you take ownership. 



1. Customer Experience

• Provide
/chat-based support for our customers across our channels (Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale)

• Answer order and product questions, resolve issues promptly, and ensure customers feel heard and supported

• Support wholesale partners with order updates, invoicing, and coordination



2. Order Management & Platform Coordination

• Manage, monitor, and troubleshoot orders across Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale channels

• Troubleshoot and resolve shipping or fulfillment issues with partners and customers

• Maintain accurate product listings, inventory data, and logistics information across platforms



3. Executive & Admin Support

• Manage founder and team inboxes, schedule meetings, and organize internal documentation

• Assist with travel bookings, meeting preparation, and note-taking

• Support small projects by tracking timelines, managing tasks, and ensuring follow-through



4. Special Projects & Company Operations

• Conduct research projects to support decision-making (tools, systems, competitors)

• Support lead generation efforts through identifying new wholesale prospects, brand partners, or influencers

• Support planning and execution of events, expos, or marketing campaigns — manage timelines, logistics, and tasks

• Document and maintain SOPs for operational clarity and training

• Test and troubleshoot web pages, workflows, or customer journeys to ensure quality and functionality

• Take ownership of ad hoc “figure it out” tasks — we’ll guide you, but we value initiative and independent problem-solving mindset



You’ll be trained on all platforms and tools, and we’ll support your learning — but we’re looking for someone who’s excited to take initiative and build systems along the way. This role evolves — as you grow, so will your responsibilities.







ABOUT YOU / REQUIRED SKILLS OR EXPERIENCE:

We value curiosity, reliability, and the ability to take ownership. If you enjoy figuring things out, learning on the job, and taking initiative, you’ll thrive here. This role is not a fit if you need constant direction or struggle to manage your own time.



Must have experience:

• 2+ years of experience in a customer service or operations support role 
/chat-based)

• 1+ years experience in eCommerce environments (Shopify, Amazon, etc.)

• Fluent in written and spoken English



Key traits:

• Strong detail orientation, organizational, and time-management skills — you’re detail-obsessed and on top of things

• Proactive problem-solver — you take initiative, do the research, and follow through

• Able to work independently, ask questions when needed, and communicate clearly

• Comfortable with constructive feedback and direct, respectful communication



Optional but a big plus:

• Experience coordinating projects, events, or marketing campaigns is a plus

• Experience with executive assistant work 
/calendar/travel management)

• Experience improving or documenting processes (SOPs)

• Experience at a startup or small business where you wore multiple hats

• “Experience coordinating projects, events, or marketing campaigns is a plus”







PAY, WORKING HOURS, HOW TO APPLY

If this description seems like it’s too intimidating for you, this position is probably not for you. However, if you’re still here reading and this sounds exciting for you, we’d love to meet you!



The full time monthly salary will be $
based on your skills and level of relevant experience. Being a member of our team has other benefits including up to 16 days of paid time off per year.



We understand you may have a busy schedule, so we’ve made our interview process extremely quick and efficient. 



To apply, please follow the steps below:

1. Get started with a quick Initial Screening here for us to get to know you: 

2. Complete the screening and we’ll be in touch with next steps once we receive it.





The position is on a first come first review basis, so the sooner you complete the screening, the more likely you'll get hired.



Good luck, and we hope to have someone awesome like you to join us!

title: Junior Video Editor (Pre-Editing Only) – On-Call Side Gig
description:

Your job is simple: clean up the footage so the main edit is faster later. I’m not looking for fancy edits. I need someone fast, organized, clean and consistent.

* I send footage when available

* You accept the task if you are free

* Usually small batches of videos

* Clean up audio

title: Sales & Booking Specialist
description:
***To proceed with the application process, please upload your resume into this Google form:
 and select the role.***



Role Summary

As a Sales & Booking Specialist, you are responsible for converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and direct channels.



Your core objective is to maximize conversion rate, occupancy, and revenue through fast response, strong sales communication, and effective follow-up.



This is a performance-driven sales role, not just customer service. You are expected to actively guide guests through the booking process and drive results.



Work Schedule

 Standard: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Pacific Time

 Flexibility required: occasional shifts in Philippines Standard Time based on business needs



Key Responsibilities

1. Lead Conversion & Sales



 Respond to guest inquiries across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and other platforms in a timely manner

 Identify guest needs and recommend suitable properties

 Use persuasive communication to highlight property features, amenities, and location advantages

 Address objections and remove booking friction

 Proactively follow up with leads to maximize conversion



2. Revenue & Listing Performance



 Take ownership of booking performance for assigned listings

 Monitor key metrics:

 Traffic

 Inquiry volume

 Conversion rate

 Occupancy



 Identify underperforming listings and recommend improvements (pricing, photos, positioning)

 Collaborate with marketing and pricing teams on optimization strategies



3. Booking Management & Guest Coordination



 Guide guests through the booking process and ensure all information is clearly communicated

 Coordinate with internal teams (operations, cleaning, maintenance) to ensure readiness

 Maintain high response rate and fast response time

 Assist with booking modifications and guest coordination



4. Performance Tracking & Reporting



 Track and report:

 Lead volume

 Inquiry-to-booking conversion rate

 Occupancy rate

 Revenue per listing



 Provide insights and recommendations to improve booking performance



Description



Month2Month is a fast-growing hospitality and property management company focused on maximizing occupancy and delivering exceptional guest experiences.

As a  Sales & Booking Specialist, you will play a critical role in driving revenue by converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across multiple platforms including Airbnb, VRBO, and Zillow.

This role is highly execution-driven and performance-focused. You will work directly on the front line of our booking operations, responding to leads, guiding guests through the reservation process, and ensuring listings perform at a high level.

This is not a passive customer service role. Success in this position requires a strong sales mindset, fast response time, attention to detail, and the ability to actively follow up and close bookings.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams including operations, pricing, and marketing to continuously improve listing performance and maximize occupancy.



Required Qualifications



 Bachelor’s degree or above

 Experience in property management, short-term rental, hospitality, or related industry

 Fluent in English with strong written communication skills

 Strong sales mindset with ability to convert leads into bookings

 Ability to multitask and manage multiple inquiries simultaneously

 Strong sense of urgency and responsiveness

 Comfortable working in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

 Basic proficiency with spreadsheets and digital tools



Preferred Qualifications

 2+ years of experience in short-term rental, OTA platforms, or sales roles

 Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, or similar platforms

 Proven track record in lead conversion or sales performance

 Familiarity with PMS systems and booking workflows

 Ability to analyze listing performance and identify improvement opportunities

 Experience working with remote and cross-functional teams



***To proceed with the application process, please upload your resume into this Google form:
 and select the role.***

title: YouTube AI Content Creator and Manager
description:
We are seeking a YouTube AI Content Creator who can leverage AI Agent tools to research, collect data, and transform insights into engaging, high-quality videos. The ideal candidate will have a strong sense of storytelling, visual creativity, and hands-on experience in video editing and channel management.



Key Responsibilities

* Use AI Agent tools to automate workflows, streamline research, and organize data efficiently

* Handle graphic design, scriptwriting, footage collection, AI voiceovers, video editing, and background music integration

* Create eye-catching thumbnails, SEO-optimized descriptions, and tags to boost visibility and engagement

* Set up and manage a YouTube channel, including profile design, cover art, logo, intro/outro, and ongoing content updates

* Ensure all content complies with YouTube’s policies and community guidelines



If you’re a creative professional who can blend AI innovation with YouTube storytelling, we’d love to work with you.

- Consistent Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM Australian Eastern Standard Time (Melbourne).

- A Supportive and Growing Team: Be part of a passionate team that values your contributions.

- Comprehensive Training: We'll equip you with the knowledge and skills you need to succeed.



As you review the description, we welcome any questions you may have about the position or our team. This position is 40 hours per week. All possibilities for raises based upon work performance.

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________

What We're Looking For:



We can teach Skills, but we can't teach Attitude.

Here are the things we're looking for:



- Exceptional English Proficiency (C2 Level is a MUST): Your spoken and written English skills are outstanding, allowing you to communicate clearly, confidently, and professionally, as you will need to read and understand basic dental terminology.

- Proven Call Centre Experience (ESSENTIAL): You have a solid background in handling phone-based customer interactions.

- Integrity. You are a good fit if you can be honest with your fellow teaUpgrade to see actual infombers. Open communication and honesty are paramount to myself and the team. If you aren't able to hit a deadline or made a mistake, by upfront about it, we are all human after all.

- Commitment. We're looking for someone who is looking for a long term relationship with the expectations that this will become full time. If you're just looking for a few months of work, this is not for you.

- Desire to Learn. We are a small team, but growing fast. We want people who are open to learning new skills and taking on new projects. If you're not excited about improving your skills, then this is not for you.

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________

If this sounds like you, please apply below and I will personally read your application, work samples and answer any questions you may have.

In your proposal, please start your subject line with 'Elevate Dental - CCR' so that I know you actually read the complete job description.

Please also send a voice recording for your application to be considered.



Thank you for your time!

Judith

title: Social Media Virtual Assistant - Nomad Cruise
description:
Join the team behind Nomad Cruise, a floating conference that brings 300+ digital nomads together for community, learning, and adventure across the world's oceans.



About Nomad Cruise

Nomad Cruise is more than an event - it's a global community. We host immersive cruises that combine inspiring talks, workshops, and unforgettable on-shore adventures, creating deep connections between remote professionals from all over the world. Our core values are community, adventure, and inspiration, and everything we do is designed to reflect them.



The Role

We're looking for a Social Media VA who genuinely enjoys the craft of social media- someone who understands tone, and keeps things consistent. You'll be the voice behind our channels day-to-day, helping us stay active, engaged, and on-brand.

Your work will reach thousands of nomads around the world and directly shape how they experience the Nomad Cruise community online.



What You'll Do



Video & Content Editing

Edit short-form video content (Reels, Stories, clips) and assist with graphics or visual assets — taking raw material from the team and turning it into polished, publish-ready content.



Community Management

Respond to comments, DMs, and mentions across Instagram and other platforms — keeping our community warm, active, and engaged in a tone that feels human and on-brand.



Content Scheduling & Coordination

Help plan and schedule posts using our content calendar, coordinating with the team to keep channels consistent and active.



Caption Writing & Ideation

Write captions, suggest content ideas, and repurpose existing material to keep our feeds fresh and our voice consistent.





The Details:

Part-time, remote

5–15 hours per week

Flexible schedule

Long-term opportunity - with the right fit, we're looking for someone to grow with us



Why You'll Love It



Work with a globally minded, purpose-driven team

Your content reaches nomads and travellers worldwide

Fully remote and flexible schedule

Gain behind-the-scenes insight into a unique international event and community



This Role Is For You If You're…



A natural communicator with a friendly, professional tone

Organised and consistent - you don't let things slip

Experienced with short-form video editing (Reels, CapCut, Premiere, or similar)

Familiar with Instagram,
, and basic scheduling tools

Comfortable working independently and managing your own time

Proactive - you flag issues and bring ideas without being asked



How to Apply

Please complete our short application form to get started. As part of your application, include a media kit or portfolio with examples of your work - edited videos or Reels, accounts you've managed, or content you've created.

We'll also ask you to record a short 1-minute video telling us what excites you about this role. No need for perfection - we just want to get a sense of who you are.



APPLICATION FORM: 
/appu6qUYAEkAjR6NM/pagR4OzgN2kbRRH3O/form



Looking forward to hearing from you,

Emilia

Project & Partnership Manager, Nomad Cruise

title: Real Estate Media QA/QC & Delivery Specialist
description:
I am a solo real estate media owner and operator starting to expand my business from myself to other photographers and support staff. I am looking for a highly detail-oriented Quality Assurance (QA) and Delivery Specialist. You will be the final "set of eyes" before our clients receive their photos.



The Workflow: 

1.) I will upload RAW HDR brackets from the field throughout the day (CST). You will be responsible for:

Downloading from AutoHDR. 

2.) Performing final QA/QC and "Flavor" edits in Adobe Lightroom (Verticals, White Balance, Sky Replacements, cleaning up clutter like wires or extra boxes in a garage.)

3.)Using AI platforms like Nano Banana Pro to prompt the AI to virtually stage, sky replace, etc. 

4.)Final Export and Delivery to clients via the Spiro platform.



The Shift: This is a fixed shift starting at 3:00 PM CST (5:00 AM PHT). This is an early morning shift for you, allowing for real-time collaboration with me while I am in the field.



The goal is to transition myself away from delivering photos to clients late at night to actual same day delivery. This will free up more time for me to focus on sales or more photos shoots. 



Requirements:

Must have a high-speed internet connection (50Mbps+).

High proficiency in Adobe Lightroom (Classic/CC).

Experience with Spiro or similar Real Estate delivery platforms is a massive plus.

Extreme attention to detail—you hate crooked lines and "blown-out" windows.

Ability to work independently and meet 2-hour turnaround times for live uploads.

title: Experienced, Xero-Proficient Management Accountant
description:
Job Description

Company: Priority Haulage Limited

Job Title: Management Accountant

Job Type: Remote / Part-Time

Reports to: Financial Controller

Start Date: May 2026



About Priority Haulage

Priority Haulage is a reputable and reliable heavy machinery and equipment transport business based in Auckland, New Zealand. We specialise in the safe and efficient transportation of a wide range of civil machinery and heavy access equipment. 



Our company motto, “Your Assets, Our Priority”, reflects our commitment to the secure and dependable movement of our clients’ valuable machinery. We take pride in delivering a high level of client satisfaction through trusted service and operational excellence.



Job Summary

Priority Haulage is seeking a capable and detail-oriented Management Accountant (Part-Time) to support the delivery of accurate and timely month-end financial reporting.



Working closely with the Financial Operations Manager and reporting to the Financial Controller, this role is responsible for maintaining financial integrity across core accounting processes, ensuring reliable data to support operational decision-making within a fast-paced, growing transport business.



This position is suited to someone highly proficient in Xero, comfortable working independently, and experienced in structured month-end close environments.



Job Responsibilities Overview

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role centred on the monthly financial close. The primary responsibility is to ensure all financial data is processed, reconciled, and reported accurately and on time each month.



The role requires availability for 1–2 days prior to month-end for close preparation, and approximately 8 working days post month-end to complete the close cycle. This is expected to equate to approximately 15–25 hours per month, with specific scheduling aligned to month-end requirements and agreed with the successful candidate. 



The role requires strong technical accounting capability, attention to detail, and a disciplined approach to financial operations. Performance is measured by the accuracy and timeliness of reporting, adherence to internal processes, and the ability to consistently deliver high-quality outputs with minimal supervision.



Over time, there may be opportunities to contribute to financial analysis, system/process improvements, and data alignment initiatives, based on capability and demonstrated performance.



Job Responsibilities

•	Prepare and analyse monthly management accounts using Xero and CCH Workpapers, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and timeliness. 

•	Perform key account reconciliations and resolve discrepancies prior to month-end close.

•	Ensure all financial activities comply with New Zealand accounting standards and tax requirements.

•	Maintain accurate financial records, filing systems, and data integrity in line with company standards 

•	Contribute to continuous improvement of financial processes, with a focus on efficiency and consistency.



Required Experience & Capability

Applicants should demonstrate proven experience operating in a structured, deadline-driven finance environment.



•	Minimum 7+ years’ hands-on accounting experience.

•	CPA qualification required, preferably a Regular Member of PICPA, with a recognised tertiary qualification in accounting.

•	Proven experience preparing monthly management accounts, conducting financial analysis, and supporting budgeting and forecasting processes.

•	Advanced proficiency in Xero is essential.

•	Strong Microsoft Excel capability, with proven ability to reconcile and analyse data using XLOOKUP/VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, pivot tables, and financial modelling functions.

•	Working knowledge of New Zealand tax regulations, accounting standards, and statutory compliance requirements.

•	Demonstrated ability to operate independently in a remote environment, with strong self-management, reliability, and a consistent track record of meeting deadlines without oversight.

•	Strong ability to collaborate cross-functionally, supporting finance-related workflows across the business.

•	High level of technical proficiency, including the Microsoft platform, with working familiarity in modern business tools such as:

o	Notion

o	ChatGPT



Preferred Experience 

•	Experience within New Zealand or Australian Chartered Accounting firms, including use of CCH Workpapers.

•	Exposure to a commercial business environment, ideally within transport, logistics, or fleet-based operations.

•	A relevant tertiary qualification in finance, business administration, or a related field.



Personal Attributes

•	Highly organised and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple responsibilities reliably.

•	Strong communication and interpersonal skills, able to operate autonomously while contributing effectively in a remote team environment.

•	Analytical and problem-solving capability, particularly in identifying, investigating, and resolving financial discrepancies.

•	Proactive and improvement-oriented, with the ability to adapt, optimise workflows, and navigate operational change.

•	Professional, client-focused mindset, demonstrating integrity, quality standards, and accountability in delivery.



Required Equipment and Setup

•	Dedicated Workspace: Quiet, distraction-free home office suitable for video calls and focused work.

•	Computer Requirements (Desktop PC or Laptop):

o	Processor: Intel Core i5 or better.

o	RAM: Minimum 8GB (16GB preferred).

o	Operating System: Windows 10/11, compatible with required business software.

o	Internet: Stable high-speed connection (minimum 10 Mbps download/upload).

•	Monitor Setup: Dual monitors preferred, or a laptop paired with an external monitor.

•	Headset: Noise-cancelling for clear communication during virtual meetings.

•	Webcam: High-quality external or integrated camera for video calls.

•	Power Backup: Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) or generator ensuring at least 3 hours of operational uptime during outages.

•	Internet Backup: Secondary internet connection (e.g., mobile data or alternative wireless) for uninterrupted connectivity.



Application Requirement

In your application submission, include the word “Motivated!” in the title.

title: React Native Mobile App Developer  (Long-Term)
description:
Wage: $10/hr | 30 hours/week | Paid weekly via Remitly



?? This is NOT a greenfield build. The app already exists and is in active development. I am looking for a reliable, skilled React Native developer who can take ownership of their work, write clean production-ready code, and help get GoShopBlack to launch.



? No major refactors

? No new features unless explicitly approved

? No “I rewrote everything” approaches



About GoShopBlack:

GoShopBlack is a marketplace mobile app that connects users with Black-owned businesses. The app is built in React Native with a Supabase backend and has draft builds on both Google Play and the Apple App Store. I do regular code reviews via GitHub and expect PRs to be tested, clean, and production-ready before submission. This is a long-term position for the right person — not a one-off project.



What You'll Be Working On:

- Ongoing React Native development for iOS & Android

- Search and filter system by location, category, and business type

- Google Maps API integration (not yet built — this is a key upcoming feature)

- Business profiles (hours, contact info, website links, reviews)

- Customer ratings and review system

- Business submission and admin approval flow

- E-commerce integration for businesses that sell online

- Social login 
 & Gmail) plus
/password auth

- Phase 2: push notifications, in-app messaging, ad integration, AI recommendations



What I'm Looking For:

- Strong hands-on React Native experience — you must show me apps you have shipped to the App Store and Google Play

- Strong Supabase experience — this is our backend, no Firebase, Node.js, or AWS

- Google Maps API integration experience

- GitHub proficiency — include your GitHub profile in your application

- Clean, well-structured code with no demo or test code in PRs

- Someone who self-tests thoroughly and does full end-to-end testing before submitting a PR

- Strong communicator who gives daily updates and flags issues early

- Weekly proof of progress required (Git commits, test builds, or screen recordings)

- Willingness to use Hubstaff for time tracking from day one

- Willingness to sign an NDA and contract before starting

- Fluent written and spoken English



How We Work:

- 30 hours per week

- Daily check-ins and progress updates required

- PRs must be fully tested and production-ready before submission

- Time tracked via Hubstaff

- Payment sent weekly via Remitly



Screening Questions (answer all 5 in your application):

1. You are taking over an existing React Native app mid-project. What are the first 3 things you check before making any code changes, and why?

2. If you had 2 weeks to prepare an existing mobile app for launch, what would you prioritize — and what would you intentionally NOT touch? Explain your reasoning.

3. Describe one project where you inherited a messy, incomplete, or poorly documented app and successfully shipped it. What was broken and what did you do first?

4. How do you prove weekly progress on an existing project? Be specific (commits, builds, test links, screen recordings, etc.)

5. What React Native state management approaches have you used in production apps, and how do you d
 which one to use?



How to Apply:

Start your message with the word Love so I know you read this fully. Then answer all 5 screening questions above and include:

- Links to React Native apps you have built and shipped

- Your GitHub profile link

- Your availability and when you can start



Applications that do not start with Love, skip the screening questions, or do not include a portfolio and GitHub link will not be considered.

description:
Before applying, please watch this video:

e91db



We are seeking a detail-oriented Accountant & Bookkeeper to manage both day-to-day bookkeeping and core accounting functions for our organization. This role is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, supporting month-end close, and coordinating with external tax professionals.



This position is ideal for someone who enjoys working directly in the books while also applying accounting knowledge to ensure accuracy, compliance, and reliable financial reporting.



Key Responsibilities:

- Maintain accurate financial records in QuickBooks for multiple properties, LLCs, and business units

- Review, interpret, and properly record HUD settlement statements and closing documents

- Record and categorize financial transactions across multiple accounts and entities

- Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable

- Process invoices, payments, and expense reimbursements

- Maintain organized documentation and supporting records

- Perform regular bank and credit card reconciliations

- Prepare schedules and reports to support tax filings

- Track deductible expenses and maintain audit-ready records

- Assist with sales tax, payroll tax, or other compliance filings as applicable



Qualifications:

- Required: Hands-on bookkeeping/accounting experience in U.S. real estate, property management, or real estate investing

- Required: Experience recording and interpreting HUD/closing statements

- Strong proficiency in QuickBooks with real estate-focused accounting workflows

- Knowledge of U.S.-based rental income flows, vendor billing practices, and property expense tracking

- Experience managing vendor payments, service providers, and maintenance-related invoices

- Experience with multi-entity or multi-LLC bookkeeping

- Strong attention to detail, accuracy, and organizational skills

- Ability to work independently, prioritize deadlines, and maintain orderly records

- Familiarity with Profit First methodology is a plus

- Accounting or bookkeeping certifications (QuickBooks ProAdvisor, etc.) preferred

title: WordPress Developer — Reverse Proxy Blog Migration
description:
WordPress Developer — Reverse Proxy Blog Migration

Squarespace to WordPress on WP Engine

Overview

We need an experienced WordPress developer to migrate a blog (~850 pages) from Squarespace to WordPress on WP Engine. The main website stays on Squarespace — only the blog moves. A Cloudflare Worker reverse proxy will route blog traffic (/blog/*) to WordPress while all other traffic continues to Squarespace. All existing blog URLs must remain identical after migration.

This is not a standard WordPress build. The reverse proxy routing is the core technical challenge.

Scope of Work

1. WP Engine Setup. Provision WordPress environment, configure theme, install required plugins. Set up permalink structure to match existing blog URLs exactly. The site has two URL patterns: flat slugs (/blog/post-slug) and date-based paths (/blog/2024/10/19/post-slug). Both must resolve correctly.

2. Content Migration. Export content from Squarespace (WXR format), import into WordPress. Write and run scripts to download all images from Squarespace’s CDN, upload to WordPress media library, and rewrite all image URLs in post content. Verify all 850+ post slugs match the originals.

3. Cloudflare Worker & DNS. Move domain nameservers to Cloudflare. Write and deploy a Cloudflare Worker that routes /blog/* requests to WP Engine and passes all other requests through to Squarespace. Configure proper Host headers, X-Forwarded-Proto, and SSL end-to-end.

4. WordPress Proxy Configuration. Configure 
p for reverse proxy operation (WP_HOME, WP_SITEURL, forwarded headers). Ensure WordPress generates correct URLs through the proxy. No redirect loops, no mixed content.

5. URL Verification & QA. Verify every migrated URL returns HTTP 200 through the proxy. Test for redirect loops, broken images, mixed content warnings, and correct canonical URLs. Deliver a QA report covering all pages.

Required Experience

•  Cloudflare Workers — you’ve written and deployed them before, not just used Cloudflare for DNS

•  Reverse proxy configuration — routing subdirectory traffic to a different origin server

•  WP Engine hosting — familiar with their portal, domain config, caching, and SFTP/SSH access

•  WordPress 
p for proxy setups — forwarded headers, WP_HOME vs WP_SITEURL

•  Large-scale content migration — 850+ pages including scripted image re-hosting

•  SSL/HTTPS configuration across proxied setups

•  WP-CLI proficiency

Project Details

Budget: $1,500–$2,500 fixed price (open to discussion based on experience)

Timeline: 3–4 weeks

Type: Contract / project-based, with potential for ongoing WordPress maintenance

Communication: Async via Slack or
. Weekly progress updates. You’ll work directly with our SEO lead who will provide technical direction and review work at each phase.



To Apply

Please include:

1. A specific example of a reverse proxy or Cloudflare Worker project you’ve completed. Describe the setup, not just “I’ve done it.”

2. A specific example of a large content migration (100+ pages), ideally Squarespace to WordPress.

3. Your availability to start and estimated completion timeline.

4. Your fixed-price quote for the full scope described above.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
WANTED: The World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative

Global | Remote | Healthcare & Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)



Note: DO NOT apply via OnlineJobs.ph. Please submit your application to 
 application that does not follow this instruction, as well as the steps outlined under “How to Apply,” will be automatically filtered out.



If you don’t love the grind, the scoreboard, and the win, do not apply.

We help independent healthcare practices (cardiology, nephrology, primary care, etc.) add predictable recurring revenue through remote patient monitoring — without adding more work to their already burnt-out staff.

Doctors are overwhelmed. Staff are drowning. Reimbursements are shrinking.

Our solution gives them lifesaving revenue and better care for their sickest patients. Your mission as our Sales Development Representative (SDR):

Turn cold contacts into hot, qualified, ready-to-buy opportunities — consistently, predictably, and at volume.

If you’re the kind of person who gets a dopamine hit from booking a qualified meeting, loves hearing “yes” more than you hate hearing “no,” and wants to be the engine of pipeline growth, keep reading.

If you just want a chill hourly gig and hate metrics... this will feel like punishment. Close this tab.



Why This Role Actually Matters

We are demand constrained, not delivery constrained.

When we get in front of the right practices, we win — and we keep them. But someone has to:

• Cut through the noise.

• Get past gatekeepers.

• Start real conversations with senior decision-makers at healthcare practices.

• Set high-intent, show-up-ready appointments for our Sales team.

That “someone” is you.

You’re not a “junior telemarketer.”

You’re a front-line revenue creator in a highly regulated, high-impact, high-ticket environment.



What You’ll Do (High-Impact Responsibilities) This is what your scoreboard will look like:

1. Own Outreach Like an Athlete Owns Training

• Hit and exceed daily activity targets:

Minimum 50+ cold calls per day.

Minimum 35+ targeted
s per day.

2. Turn Cold Prospects Into Qualified Opportunities

• Speak to practice administrators, physician owners, and clinic leaders about

their staffing overload and revenue pain.

3. Handle Objections Like a Pro

• You will be trained — and expected — to turn these into conversations, not dead

ends.

4. Feed the Revenue Machine with Data G Feedback

• Log everything cleanly in HubSpot and Track: calls, connects, show rates,

conversion rates, and reasons for “no.”

5. Hit ?uotas — Then Break Them

• Consistently hit monthly meeting/qualified opportunity quotas.

You will not be “just setting meetings.”

You will be creating pipeline that turns into accounts and real-world clinical impact. 



What Sucks About This Job (On Purpose)

We’re going to be brutally honest:

• You will face rejection. A lot. Healthcare gatekeepers are pros. You’ll hear “not

interested,” “busy,” and voi
 more than you hear “yes.” If that crushes you,

this isn’t your game.

• High volume is non-negotiable. 50 calls and 35
s per day is the floor, not the

ceiling. “I’m more of a thinker than a doer” does not work here.

• You will be measured. Your activity, connects, meetings, show rates, and

opportunity quality will be tracked. If that stresses you out, we’ll stress each other

out.

• You must learn the healthcare world. This isn’t selling software to startups. You’ll

need to understand RPM, CPT codes at a basic level, and the pressure providers are

under.

• We move fast. Scripts, messaging, and targeting will evolve. If you cling to “the way

we’ve always done it,” you’ll be left behind.

If any of this makes you think, “That sounds intense, I just want something easy,” we are not for you.

If it makes you think, “Perfect, that’s where I outwork and outperform everyone else,” keep going.



What’s Amazing About This Job

Now the upside.

• You’re selling something genuinely valuable. This isn’t a gimmick. When we win a

practice, they get real incremental revenue and their sickest patients get better monitoring.

• Global-first, remote-first. You can work from anywhere with strong overlap to U.S. Eastern time. We care about output and attitude, not where your chair is.

• Above-market pay for true A-players.

o We benchmark compensation and aim to pay at least 10?ove the high-

water mark for top SDRs in your region.

o In emerging markets, that level of comp is life-changing — and we treat it

that way.

• Real coaching G growth. You’ll work closely with a team that knows the numbers and the craft. If you want to grow into senior SDR, AE, or sales leadership, this is a real launchpad.

• Mission + money. You get to earn great income while working on something that improves patient lives and helps struggling practices survive.



Who You Are (Non-Negotiables)

• Experience selling into healthcare is a MUST. You’ve sold to practices, hospitals,

or healthcare orgs before. You understand that world is different.

• Elite communicator — clear, confident, and human on the phone and in writing.

• Active listener. You don’t bulldoze; you hear what they’re actually saying and redirect wisely.

• Sales athlete. You love the game — the dials, the script tweaks, the scoreboard, the improvement.

• Resilient. High volume + rejection doesn’t drain you; it sharpens you.

• Organized and on top of your follow-ups, call notes, and tasks — not digging

through chaos every morning.

• Comfortable with sales tools and CRM (HubSpot) — or confident you can get up to speed quickly.

• A genuine team player: competitive, but not toxic; collaborative, not needy.

If you’ve never sold into healthcare and have no interest in learning that world, this role is

not for you.



How to Apply

We filter hard at the top of the funnel. If you can’t follow instructions here, you won’t make it in the role.

1.
 Subject Line (Exact Wording) Your subject line must be:

"I actually read the instructions."



2. Attach Your Resume (Correct Format Only)

• Attach your resume as a PDF.

• File name must be:

[lastname].[firstname].sdr.cv.pdf

Example: doe.jane.sdr.cv.pdf



3. Animal Question (In the
 Body)

Answer this in 3–5 sentences:

If you were an animal, what animal would you be and why — specifically as a Sales Development Representative?

We’re looking at how you think and communicate, not a “right” answer.



4. English Proficiency Link

Include a link to an English proficiency test result (Duolingo, IELTS, TOEFL, or similar).



5. Role-Specific Mini Task (Non-Negotiable)

In the same
, include:

• One sample cold
 (150 words max) you would send to:

A practice administrator at a cardiology clinic who is overwhelmed with staff workload and worried about declining reimbursements.

• One 3–4 bullet call opener you would use on a cold call to the same person. Plain text in the
 is fine. No design necessary. We care about clarity, relevance, and punch.



6. Send a 2-minute video on a time when you showed Perseverance in your life and/or career



7. Where to Send It

Send everything above to:





Do not reply via OnlineJobs.ph. Please submit your application to 
 that do not follow these instructions will be automatically filtered out.



What Happens Next (Including the Paid Trial)

1. We’ll review submissions that follow the instructions exactly.

2. If you look like a fit, we’ll invite you to a short interview focused on real conversations and scenarios, not generic “tell me about yourself” fluff.

3. Top candidates will be invited to a paid trial project, which may include:

o Running a small outbound sprint to a defined target list, o Using a script and messaging we co-create,

o Reporting your numbers and learnings.

This paid trial is the real evaluation, not your resume.

If you crush it, we’ll talk offer, ramp plan, and how you’re going to help us fill the pipeline

with high-value healthcare accounts.

Final CTA



If you’re:

• The kind of SDR who loves the grind, the game, and the win,

• Hungry to earn above-market pay for above-market performance,

• Excited to sell something that actually improves lives in the real world...

Then follow the instructions to the letter, send your application, and show us you’re the World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative.

title: Senior Cloud Accountant (Direct Hire / Work from Home)
description:
Join the Team as a Senior  Cloud Accountant at Bean Ninjas USA!

Philippine CPA license highly preferred.



Bean Ninjas USA is on the lookout for enthusiastic and skilled professionals to become part of our expanding team. If you're passionate about cloud accounting and meet the qualifications listed below, we’d love to connect with you!



Filipino candidate must possess the following (Please do not apply if you do not have the following):



~ 5+years of full-time accounting experience within a BPO or similar environment.

- end-to-end bookkeeping experience including preparation of Balance Sheet and Income Statement

~ Proficient in cloud accounting software and platforms.

~ Strong analytical skills with attention to detail.

~ Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

~ Bachelor's degree in Accounting or related field (preferred)

Qualifications for the role:



- Full-time work (Mon-Fri) for our US clients within the Philippines working hours of 8 am to 5 pm.

- We also offer flexibility on the schedule at work once you are familiar with the processes and client work.

- Excellent attention to detail and time management skills.

- Team player who would get along well with the rest of the Bean Ninjas team



Specific Responsibilities



In this role, you will look after management reporting and weekly/monthly transaction processing for a portfolio of Bean Ninjas bookkeeping clients.



Bookkeeping Service Delivery



-Uses Xero and QuickBooks Online

-Uses Xero and QBO add-ons and integrations like Hubdoc, A2X, Receipt Bank, Stripe, and Paypal.

-Responds to client queries via our online
 helpdesk software Helpscout.

-Communicates with other staff via Slack.

-You write well: concise, understandable, helpful, professional, and fast. You can build rapport with others solely through your writing.



Remote work

-You’re self-motivated and ideally, have previous experience working remotely and with little direct supervision.

-You must have an excellent internet connection.

-You know how to keep your computer and work secure and private.

-You know the joys of using two monitors (or splitting a big monitor) to do bookkeeping work.



Who are we?

Bean Ninjas is a bookkeeping and financial reporting business founded on Australia’s Gold Coast in 2015.



We specialize in eCommerce businesses and our mission is to create financial freedom through stress-free business finances for our clients.



We have over 16 Philippine tea
mbers here and we are growing!



What’s it like being part of the Bean Ninjas team?



Career development

We believe in developing our team and promoting internally. You will go through an initial 6-month training program and will then receive ongoing training and mentoring. We prepare individual staff development plans for each tea
mber.



Flexible and remote role

This is a remote role and you will have some level of flexibility in choosing your regular work hours, but must generally be online during Philippine working hours.



Benefits

We offer a variety of benefits from 13th-month pay, birthday vouchers, birthday time off, accrued leaves, and Christmas shutdown leave (where everyone does not need to log in to work for a couple of days during the Christmas shutdown which is usually 5 to 7 working days, yet these days are still paid for by the company!), 6-week maternity leave, remote social activities, and pieces of training and up to date certifications for our accountants on various accounting platforms or integrations.



Bean Ninjas is recognized as an industry leader. You will be working alongside Chartered Accountants and CPAs who have come from firms such as Ernst and Young, BDO, and Grant Thornton.



We won the Xero Australia Bookkeeping Partner of the Year Award in 2019 and the equivalent state award for Queensland in both 2017 and 2019.



Applications can be sent to 
 and include a copy of your resume. Please do not apply if you have no relevant experience or are only looking for a project-based role. Thank you!

title: Shopify & Amazon Store Operations Specialist
description:
Baie Botanique is an award-winning natural skincare brand selling internationally through Shopify and Amazon.

We are looking for a highly organised E-commerce Operations Specialist to support the day-to-day running of our Shopify stores and Amazon marketplaces and ensure everything operates smoothly.



Salary: 35,000 PHP



We are a small, experienced team looking for a reliable long-term tea
mber to grow with us..





**IMPORTANT:**

You must be comfortable working **UK hours (8am – 5pm Monday to Friday)**





What a Typical Week in This Role Looks Like



In this role, your week will involve a mix of operational tasks across our Shopify and Amazon stores.

Typical activities include:



• Checking Shopify and Amazon stores to ensure listings, pricing and content are accurate

• Monitoring customer orders and coordinating with fulfilment centres when needed

• Updating product pages, collections and website content using Shopify

• Tracking inventory levels across fulfilment locations

• Supporting improvements to product pages and store layout

• Responding to occasional customer
s relating to orders or deliveries (customer volume is generally low)

• Helping document operational processes as the business grows



Most tasks are operational and process-driven, with clear systems and support from the team.



1. Store & Marketplace Management



• Ensuring Shopify and Amazon listings are accurate and up to date

• Adding new products and maintaining existing listings

• Updating product pages, collections and site content

• Improving product page clarity and layout

• Troubleshooting issues with listings or pages



2. Orders, Fulfilment & Inventory



• Monitoring orders across Shopify and Amazon

• Communicating with fulfilment centres when needed

• Tracking inventory across locations

• Coordinating stock transfers between UK, EU and US fulfilment centres

• Reviewing fulfilment invoices and resolving discrepancies



3. Customer & Wholesale Support



• Responding to customer
s relating to orders and deliveries

• Resolving order issues or address updates

• Supporting communication with wholesale partners where required

• Assisting with marketplace partner coordination





4. Operational Improvements



• Helping document operational processes

• Supporting improvements to workflows and reporting

• Keeping systems organised as the business grows



General Operational Support



You may occasionally assist with additional operational tasks that support the wider e-commerce team.





Key Qualities We Are Looking For



We are looking for someone who:

- Works quickly, communicates proactively, and keeps tasks moving without delays

- Has hands-on experience with Shopify and/or Amazon, not just theoretical or support-level work

- Takes ownership of tasks and follows through without needing constant direction

- Is highly organised and detail-oriented

- Is comfortable working with product data, inventory tracking, and spreadsheets

- Can work independently and solve problems without step-by-step instruction



Benefits

• Full-time long-term remote position

• Work directly with an established international skincare brand

• Annual skincare allowance

• UK public holidays off

• Additional 5 days annual leave

• Stable working hours (UK time: 8am–5pm Monday–Friday)

• Opportunity to grow with the company as our e-commerce operations expand



You should already be comfortable working inside Shopify and ideally have some experience with Amazon.



Application Instructions

Please include the following in your application:

• Your CV (PDF preferred)

• A short introduction describing your experience with Shopify and Amazon

- 2 examples of Shopify stores or pages you have worked on



At the start of your application message, please include the word:

BOTANICAL

This helps us confirm that you have read the full job description.



Shortlisted candidates will be invited to record a 2–3 minute Loom video introducing themselves and discussing their Shopify and Amazon experience.

title: Google Ads Strategist
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing digital marketing agency focused exclusively on the self storage industry. Our mission is to help storage facility owners maximize occupancy and revenue through high-performance digital marketing strategies.



With increasing demand for paid acquisition, we are expanding our team and looking for a Google Ads expert who can own and scale PPC performance across multiple client accounts.



Role Overview



We are seeking a highly skilled Google Ads Strategist to lead, optimize, and scale paid search campaigns for our self storage clients. This role is both strategic and execution-focused — ideal for someone who understands how to drive measurable ROI through data-driven decision-making.



You will be responsible for managing multiple accounts, improving conversion performance, and continuously identifying growth opportunities.



Key Responsibilities

Own end-to-end management of Google Ads campaigns (Search, Display, Performance Max)

Develop and execute high-performing PPC strategies tailored to self storage markets

Conduct keyword research, bid strategy optimization, and audience targeting

Optimize campaigns for lead generation and occupancy growth (CPL, conversion rate, ROAS)

Continuously A/B test ad copy, landing pages, and campaign structures

Analyze performance data and provide actionable insights to clients and internal teams

Manage budgets efficiently across multiple accounts

Stay current with Google Ads updates, trends, and best practices



Required Qualifications

3+ years of hands-on Google Ads experience (agency experience preferred)

Proven track record of driving measurable ROI through paid search

Deep understanding of keyword strategy, bidding models, and conversion tracking

Strong analytical mindset with experience using Google Analytics, Tag Manager, and reporting tools

Experience managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

Ability to translate data into clear insights and action plans



Preferred (Nice-to-Have) Skills

SEO expertise (on-page and technical fundamentals)

Experience with
 / Meta Ads and other paid social platforms

Familiarity with local service-based marketing (self storage, real estate, home services, etc.)

Landing page optimization and CRO experience

Experience with call tracking and lead attribution tools

title: Online Installations Technician
description:
As an Online Installation Technician, you will play a critical role in remotely supporting the installation of AI systems in the U.S. This is a fully remote contractor position where you will guide on-the-ground technicians through various installation steps, ensuring successful implementation and troubleshooting of any issues that arise. Your expertise in hardware, software, and troubleshooting will be essential in maintaining smooth operations and delivering high-quality support to our partners and customers. Payment for this role is on an hourly basis.



You would bring:

- Excellent Communication Skills: Proficient in English with an easy-to-understand accent, confidence in speaking, and smooth communication skills.

- Technical Equipment: Dedicated work PC with a fast and stable internet connection, sufficient RAM, and adequate memory.

- Experience: Proven experience in handling communication in English, including calls and interactions with various business partners.

- Technical Expertise: Familiarity with hardware, software, and troubleshooting. This role requires candidates who can handle technical tasks beyond basic help, such as adjusting laptop brightness or taking screenshots.

- Stress Management: Ability to remain calm and take control of situations under stress.

- Self-Learning Ability: A fast learner with the capability to research and troubleshoot issues independently before seeking assistance.

- Organizational Skills: Well-organized and detail-oriented approach to work.

- Full-Time Commitment: Willingness to dedicate full time to the role, with the opportunity for growth and development within the company.

- Flexibility: Ability to work long shifts when needed, with the understanding that Field Engineers (FEs) may need to stay late until the job is done.



About the role:

- Remote Position: This is a fully remote job where you will work from your location, managing installations and support virtually.

- Collaboration: Work closely with on-site technicians to complete AI system installations, including troubleshooting issues during the process. You will guide the on-the-ground technician through various installation steps.

- Communication: Effectively communicate with external personnel from our partners and customers as needed.

- Task Management: Handle installation-related tasks assigned by the company.



Please fill out the form so we can get started!

title: Customer Support (Written) | No Experience Needed | FUN| Dance Academy
description:
***READ FULL JOB POST BEFORE APPLYING. *** Please don't waste your time or ours. Thanks :)



Full-time, long-term opportunity.



Four days per week, or five days per week scheduling options.



Looking for:

- Outstanding English

- Empathy



Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL.



ABOUT US

We are the largest salsa & bachata dance academy in Los Angeles and are growing rapidly.



The culture here is FUN, fast, and friendly. Our goal is to make every student feel welcome and completely taken care of.



Dancing changes lives - we are on a mission to deliver the best salsa/bachata educational experience on Earth.



The company is MaS Dance Academy.





ABOUT YOUR TEAM

Working with a remote team that helps with all facets of the business, you'll form a close working relationship and bond with your team mates. We even host team retreats in the Philippines!



----------------------------

SCHEDULE

All times written in PST (Los Angeles time)



TRAINING (2-4 weeks):

Sunday - Thursday 9am - 6pm (with 1 hour break)



AFTER TRAINING



Both options are full-time 40 hours per week.



OPTION 1 - FIVE DAY SCHEDULE:

Thursday - Monday 8am - 5pm (with 1 hour break)



OPTION 2 - FOUR DAY SCHEDULE:

Saturday, Tuesday, Wednesday 10am - 9pm (with 1 hour break)

Friday 12pm (noon) - 11pm (with 1 hour break)

----------------------------



YOUR ROLE

• Your primary role is written student support (text,
 etc.)

• When there are no support tasks to complete, you'll contribute in a wide variety of other ways, depending on your skills and interests. Some examples may include writing blogs, updating websites, doing research, collecting and analyzing numerical data and using it to create reports, etc.





WRITTEN VS PHONE SUPPORT

• 95% of our student support is written. However, occasionally you will need to be on the phone with a student. You must be comfortable on the phone.





TYPES OF SUPPORT YOU'LL HANDLE

• One on one texting and
ing

• Mass texting and
ing

• Occasional phone calls (rare)

• Updating database after every interaction and attempted interaction

• Daily reports

• Etc.





COMPANY CULTURE

Working in a small business like ours is very different from a large corporation. Some of the differences are:



• Personal tone: Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL. It's like talking to your best friend. We use emoji's and exclamation points and are very personal. We take the opposite approach of large corporations that give robotic, frustrating, not very helpful support. You need to be intuitive and have strong emotional intelligence to feel what the students are needing when they communicate.



• More creativity: there is more freedom to adjust your responses for each scenario. This is more challenging than just following strict templates, but also more stimulating and exciting.



• Impact: your actions will have a big impact on the business, which you will clearly see



• Feedback: your feedback on what we are doing well, and what could be better, is not only valued, but is mandatory! We want everyone here thinking as if they were the business owner.





ABOUT YOU

• Perfect written English

• Available full-time

• High emotional intelligence

• Flexible

• Quick learner

• Thrives in fast-paced, quickly changing environment

• Desire to always seek better ways to do things, including finding ways to use A.I. tools

• Happy to be on the phone when needed (which is rarely)

• Tremendous attention to detail - gets things right the first time

• PRO-ACTIVE. Always looking for ways to improve.

• Meticulous, organized, methodical. Almost never misses a detail.

• Critical, creative thinker. Not robotic. Always looking for better ways to do things. Always considering all the angles of a task.

• Not afraid to share your opinion - we NEED to hear your opinion on the approaches that we take so that we can improve.

• Able to ask clarifying questions in order to get tasks right the first time

• Strong communicator

• Makes decisions independently

• Quick learner

• Fast computer

• Fast, dependable Internet

• Bonus if you have experience or interest in salsa or bachata or any dance!





APPLY NOW. FORMAT YOUR APPLICATION AS A NUMBERED LIST WITH THE FOLLOWING:

1. A brief intro about yourself



2. Are you happy to be on the phone when needed?



3. This is a fast-paced environment with quickly changing conditions. You’ll need to be able to think quickly on your feet and track dozens of moving pieces at once with precision and accuracy. This role is NOT for people who feel overly anxious in this type of environment. Apply only if you thrive in such an environment. title: Executive Assistant to Founder – Sales & Marketing Operations
description:
We are an Australian business in the window and door industry looking for a highly capable Executive Assistant to Founder who can help take the day-to-day load of sales, marketing, admin, and business support tasks off the owner.



This is not a basic admin role.



We need someone who is highly organised, productive, systems-focused, and able to think for themselves. You will work closely with the founder and help keep the business running smoothly across sales support, marketing coordination, CRM management, task management, follow-ups, and general business operations.



You must be confident using modern systems and technology, including tools such as HubSpot, Notion, Canva, AI tools, and other productivity software. We are looking for someone who is proactive, commercially aware, and able to keep things moving without needing constant reminders.



This role suits someone who wants to become a true right-hand support person in a growing business.



Key Responsibilities

Executive Assistant / Founder Support

Help manage the founder’s day-to-day workload and priorities

Keep tasks, follow-ups, and deadlines organised

Assist with inbox management, reminders, and business admin

Help keep internal systems, files, and documents organised

Support the founder with ad hoc business tasks as needed

Sales Support

Manage and update HubSpot CRM

Track leads, quotes, follow-ups, and sales activity

Build and organise prospect lists

Prepare follow-up
s and support sales outreach

Help manage pipeline activity and task reminders

Make sure opportunities are followed up and not missed

Assist with proposals, client communication, and sales admin

Marketing Support

Help organise and execute day-to-day marketing tasks

Assist with
 campaigns, LinkedIn activity, and marketing coordination

Create simple marketing materials and presentations using Canva or similar tools

Organise photos, project examples, and marketing content

Assist in keeping marketing collateral current and well presented

Support content preparation and campaign execution

Systems / Operations Support

Use Notion to manage tasks, workflows, and business organisation

Use AI tools to improve productivity and speed up tasks where appropriate

title: DTC Video Ad Editor — Health Brand (Full-Time)
description:
**Hours:** Full-time, 40 hrs/week. Flexible schedule. Must overlap 2-3 hours daily with US Eastern Time for communication.



**About the Role:**



We're a DTC health supplement brand scaling aggressively through Meta 
/Instagram) ads. Our static image ads and long-form copy are already profitable. Now we're moving into video and we need a dedicated editor who can take our written scripts and produce scroll-stopping video ads at volume.



You will NOT be writing scripts. You will NOT be managing ads. You will NOT be on camera. All copy, strategy, and talent are handled. Your one job: take scripts and brand assets and turn them into video ads that make people stop scrolling and watch.



**What You'll Be Producing:**



• AI UGC ads using HeyGen, Synthesia, or D-ID (realistic talking-head videos generated from our scripts)

• VSL (Video Sales Letter) ads with voiceover, text on screen, b-roll, and product shots

• Talking head / doctor authority ads with AI avatars delivering scripts to camera

• Podcast clip style ads (two hosts discussing a topic, waveform, captions)

• Reaction style ads (doctor reacting to a news headline or study)

• Hook variations (same video body, different first 3 seconds, multiple versions for testing)



Target output once up to speed: 15-20 video assets per week.



**Tools You Should Know (or learn quickly):**



Required:

• CapCut Pro

• Canva

• Understanding of 9:16, 1:1, 4:5 aspect ratios



Strongly Preferred:

• HeyGen, Synthesia, or D-ID (AI avatar generation)

• ElevenLabs (AI voiceover)

• DaVinci Resolve or Adobe Premiere

• AI asset generation tools for UGC, animation, b-roll, and graphics



Nice to Have:

• After Effects or basic motion graphics

• Opus Clip or similar AI clipping tools

• Experience editing for DTC, ecom, supplements, or health/wellness brands



**What We're Looking For:**



• PACING AND STORYTELLING are everything. I don't care about fancy transitions or flashy effects. If you lead with that in your portfolio, we're not a fit. I care about whether someone watches for 3 seconds or 60. Fast cuts. Pattern interrupts. Text at the right 
nt. B-roll that matches the emotional beat of the script. That's what I'm hiring for.



• You actually consume short-form content obsessively. I need someone who lives on TikTok and Instagram Reels and understands what's working RIGHT NOW in March 2026. Not someone who learned trends 6 months ago. If you can tell me what hook formats are performing this week and why, you're already ahead of 90% of applicants.



• You understand organic formats. I'm not running polished corporate ads. I need content that feels native to the feed. If someone can't tell the difference between your ad and an organic post until they see "Sponsored," that's the goal. If your portfolio is full of clean, branded, studio-quality content, this isn't the role for you.



• You know how to generate AI assets. UGC avatars, AI voiceover, AI-generated b-roll, animated graphics, product mockups. The ability to use AI tools to produce content that looks real and feels human is a core skill for this role, not a bonus.



• Speed over perfection. We test at high volume. 80% quality delivered fast beats 100% quality delivered slow. Every time. We'd rather test 15 variations this week than perfect 3 of them over two weeks.



• Visual storytelling instincts. We give you the words. You d
 what the viewer SEES while hearing those words. That visual layer is what separates a video that converts from one that gets skipped.



• Strong English, written and spoken. You'll be reading English scripts, placing English text overlays, and communicating with me daily in English. This needs to be sharp.



• Proactive communication. If something in the script doesn't work for video, tell me. If you have a better idea, share it. If you see a trending format we should test, bring it to me. I want a collaborator who's plugged in, not someone waiting for instructions.



• Reliability. Show up. Hit deadlines. Communicate if something is going to be late. Simple stuff that most people fail at.



**What You DON'T Need:**



• You don't write scripts (we do all copywriting)

• You don't manage ads (we handle media buying)

• You don't appear on camera (all talent is AI or sourced separately)

• You don't need years of agency experience — hunger, taste, and skill matter more than resume length



**Growth Opportunity:**



This brand is scaling. The right person in this role grows with it. More creative ownership. More formats to experiment with. Higher pay as revenue scales. We're looking for someone who wants to build something, not just collect a paycheck.



**To Apply — Read This Carefully:**



Send ALL THREE of the following. Applications missing any of these will not get a response.



1. A Loom video (2-3 minutes). Introduce yourself. Show your editing workspace and setup. Tell me what tools you use and what kind of content you've edited. Talk to me like a normal person, not like you're reading a script.



2. Portfolio: 2-5 examples of video ads or short-form content you've edited. DTC, ecom, or health content preferred. If you don't have those, send your best fast-paced short-form work that feels native and organic. YouTube vlogs and wedding videos don't count.



3. Answer this question in 2-3 sentences: "What makes someone stop scrolling and actually watch a video ad instead of skipping past it?"



DO NOT send just a resume. DO NOT send a generic cover letter. Loom + portfolio + answer. That's it.



Top candidates will receive a PAID test project ($20-30)  so you can show me what you can do. I don't ask anyone to work for free.



Looking forward to finding the right person for this. If this sounds like you, apply now.

title: Outbound Lead Gen Specialist — Ecommerce Agency Referrals (Base + Commission)
description:
We partner with top marketing agencies that help ecommerce brands scale on Amazon, TikTok Shop, Google Ads, and Meta Ads.



Your job: message ecommerce brand owners, get them interested, and send us the ones who respond. That's it.



You do the outreach. You do the follow-ups. When someone replies and shows interest, you add them to a shared Google Sheet with their info and the conversation screenshot. We handle everything from there.



You only send us warm leads — people who have actually responded and expressed interest. No cold names, no "maybe" leads.



## Day to Day



- Research and find ecommerce brands doing $30K+ per month on Amazon, TikTok Shop, or Shopify

- Send 50–100 personalized messages per day 
, LinkedIn, Instagram DM, TikTok DM)

- Follow up with people who don't reply (Day 3, Day 7, Day 14)

- When someone responds with interest, log them in the shared Google Sheet with: name, brand, contact info, platform they sell on, and screenshot of the conversation

- Report daily: messages sent, replies received, warm leads submitted



## Pay



- **$300 USD/month base** (paid biweekly)

- **$500 per converted lead** that signs up for Meta/TikTok ad management or creator management

- **$200 + 5% recurring for 12 months** of the client's monthly ad spend for leads that sign up for Amazon or Google ad management



The recurring commission means one good Amazon/Google lead can pay you for an entire year. A client spending $10K/month on Amazon ads = $200 upfront + $500/month for 12 months to you.



- Realistic earnings: **$1,000–$3,000+/month** with recurring commissions. 



## Requirements



- 1+ year of outbound messaging experience (cold
, LinkedIn, DM outreach)

- Strong written English — you're messaging American and Canadian business owners

- Understand what Amazon FBA, TikTok Shop, Shopify, and Meta Ads are

- Can work independently

- Reliable internet, available during North American hours (at least 4 hrs overlap with EST)



## To Apply



1. Start your message with the word "OUTBOUND"

2. Write a sample 3-sentence DM to a supplement brand owner selling on Amazon — pitch them on a free strategy call with a top ecommerce agency

3. What was your last outbound role? How many messages per day and what was your reply rate?



Skip any of these 3 steps and your application won't be reviewed.



## Schedule



Part-time (4–5 hours/day). Full-time opportunity based on results.

title: Elite Customer Service Representative (Perfect English Required, Tech-Savvy, High-Level Thinker)

We are looking for someone sharp, fast, and highly intelligent who can communicate at a level where our customers assume they are speaking with someone here in the United States. If your English is not excellent, this is not the role for you. If you are someone who learns quickly, thinks critically, and takes pride in being one of the best, keep reading.



We are a fast-growing pest control company based in Florida, and we are building a high-performance remote team. We expect professionalism, strong communication, and attention to detail at all times.



What You’ll Be Doing

 • Answering customer inquiries via phone, text, and

 • Booking jobs and managing schedules in our CRM

 • Following up with customers and keeping conversations moving

 • Handling billing questions and basic account support

 • Communicating with our internal team through Slack

 • Using tools like Google Sheets, CRM systems, and AI tools such as ChatGPT

 • Thinking critically, not just following scripts



Requirements (Read Carefully Before Applying)



1. Near-native English fluency (spoken and written)

Your communication must be clear, natural, and professional. Customers should not feel a language barrier.



2. High intelligence and problem-solving ability

You can think through situations, not just follow instructions.



3. Tech-savvy comfortable with:

 • Google Sheets

 • Slack

 • CRM systems

 • AI tools like ChatGPT

  

4. Strong attention to detail

Small mistakes are not acceptable in this role.



5. Positive, upbeat attitude

You bring energy and professionalism to every interaction.

 

6. Reliable internet and workspace



Compensation



We are willing to pay above average for the right person.

 • Starting range: $6 to $10 per hour, depending on experience and skill

 • Opportunities for growth and long-term position



How to Apply (IMPORTANT – Applications Without This Will Be Ignored)



To be considered, follow these exact steps:

 1. Start your application with the phrase:

“I am your next elite CSR.”



 2. Send a short voice recording (1–2 minutes) answering this:



“How would you handle an upset customer who says your service didn’t work?”



 3. Include a screenshot of a Google Sheet you have worked on (or create one)

 4. Briefly explain your experience with:

 • CRM systems

 • Slack

 • ChatGPT or AI tools



We are only interviewing candidates who follow instructions and demonstrate strong communication skills.

Final Note



We are building a team of high performers.

If you are average, this role is not for you.

If you take pride in being excellent, we want to meet you.
