title: Full-Time Video Editor / Content Manager (YouTube + Podcast + Short-Form)
description:
*please read the entire job post before applying*



I’m looking for a full-time content-minded video editor who is genuinely obsessed with content.



And to be clear…



I am not just looking for a “video editor.”



I’m looking for someone who can help own my content and take it from:



raw footage ? edited video ? thumbnail ? clips ? published content



In other words:



I want someone who thinks in full systems, not just someone who edits clips.



This role is for someone who wants to help manage the entire content flow, not just wait for footage and complete tasks.



If you’re the kind of person who naturally thinks:



“How do we make this video stronger?”

“How do we package this better?”

“How do we improve retention?”

“How do we turn one piece of content into multiple strong assets?”

“How do we make this whole process smoother and better?”



…then you’re probably the kind of person I’m looking for.



If video editing feels like “just a job” to you, this probably isn’t the right fit.



I want someone who eats, sleeps, and breathes content.



About Me



I create content in the mid-term rental niche, which is sort of like Airbnb / furnished rentals / real estate investing.



My content is centered around:



Mid-term rentals

Real estate investing

Entrepreneurship

Business / wealth-building



My YouTube channel currently has 3,000+ subscribers:





My Goal With Content



I want to be very clear about this:



My goal is NOT to go viral.



My goal is to create the best, most engaging content in my niche.



I’d much rather have:



1,000 highly engaged followers

than

10,000,000 random followers who don’t care



So I’m not looking for someone who just wants to make flashy edits for the sake of it.



I’m looking for someone who understands how to make content:



clean

professional

engaging

watchable

valuable

and strong for my specific audience



What You’ll Be Responsible For



This role is about much more than just editing.



I’m looking for someone who can help take content from raw to ready.



That means helping own the process from:



raw footage

to polished edits

to thumbnails

to clips

to content that is fully ready to publish



To start, your main responsibilities will include:



Editing YouTube long-form videos

Editing podcast episodes

Creating YouTube thumbnails

Repurposing content into Instagram clips / short-form content



And over time, I’d love for this role to grow into helping with things like:



YouTube SEO

content packaging

topic ideas

titles

workflow improvements

and helping increase overall content output



This is not just a “video editing” job.



This is a content role.



Current Content Workload



Right now, the workload is roughly:



1 YouTube video per week

2 podcast episodes per week

plus clipping content for Instagram / short-form



The types of videos you’d be working on include:



Talking head YouTube videos

Podcast episodes

Property walkthroughs

Screen-share / educational videos



Growth Opportunity



I also want to be transparent about this:



My goal is to increase the amount of YouTube content over time



as the right person gets more comfortable with my style, workflow, and brand.



So if this is a great fit, there is definitely room for this role to grow.



I’m not just looking for someone to “help here and there.”



I’m looking for someone who can become a real part of the content machine and help me produce more high-quality content consistently.



Long-Form Editing



You’ll be responsible for editing YouTube videos and podcast episodes in a way that makes them feel:



tighter

cleaner

more engaging

more polished

easier to watch



This may include:



pacing improvements

cuts

zooms / punch-ins

text overlays

B-roll

sound design

light motion graphics

improving overall flow and watchability



I don’t need flashy edits for the sake of flashy edits.



I care much more about:



strong pacing

clear storytelling

retention

and making the content actually enjoyable to watch



Short-Form Content



You’ll also be responsible for clipping long-form content into:



Instagram Reels / short-form clips



This includes:



finding strong 
nts

identifying good hooks

creating engaging clips

adding captions when needed

making clips feel native to short-form platforms



I want someone who can help me get more mileage out of each piece of content, not just edit one long-form video and move on.



YouTube Thumbnails



You’ll also be responsible for creating:



YouTube thumbnails from scratch



This is a big part of the role.



I want someone who understands:



curiosity

click-through rate

clean but strong design

what makes someone actually click



You do not need to create over-the-top, gimmicky thumbnails.



I care much more about:



strong packaging

good taste

and thumbnails that fit my niche and audience



What I’m REALLY Looking For



I do not want someone who needs constant hand-holding.



I’m looking for someone who:



takes ownership

thinks proactively

catches mistakes before I have to

pays attention to detail

actually cares about quality

wants to improve the content, not just “finish the task”



The ideal person for this role is someone who can eventually help me think not just like an editor…



…but like someone who is helping run the content side of the business.



If you wait to be told exactly what to do every step of the way, this won’t be the right fit.



Big Dealbreakers



A few things that are a hard no for me:



needing too much hand-holding

careless mistakes

obvious typos

sloppy work

low attention to detail

generic / low-effort edits

weak communication

missing deadlines



I care a lot about:



speed

creativity

reliability

communication

attention to detail



So yes — I’m looking for someone who is really good.



Bonus If You Also Have Experience With:



This is not required, but definitely a plus:



YouTube SEO

titles / topic ideas

audience retention strategy

content packaging

helping improve overall video performance

working with business / real estate / educational YouTube channels



Over time, I’d love for this role to become bigger than just editing.



Ideally, I want someone who can eventually help with:



what topics to post

how to package videos better

how to improve content performance

and how to make the whole content system run better

Who This Is a Great Fit For



This role is probably a great fit if:



you are obsessed with YouTube

you study content in your free time

you notice what makes videos good

you genuinely enjoy editing

you care about making content better, not just “done”

you want to be part of building something



This role is NOT a great fit if:



you need to be told every little step

you don’t care about quality

you rush through work carelessly

you’re just looking for another random client



Important: This Is a Full-Time Role



This is a full-time position (40 hours/week).



I’m looking for someone who wants to be a real part of what I’m building — not someone juggling a bunch of other clients and trying to squeeze this in.  I must be your only current client!



I’m flexible on working hours, but:



deadlines and communication are non-negotiable.



This is strictly full-time only.



How To Apply



If you apply, please send me the following:



1. Your best past work



Please send:



2–3 examples of YouTube videos you’ve edited

Any thumbnails you’ve created

Anything you’ve worked on that you think is relevant



I especially want to see your experience editing for YouTube, not just random video projects.



2. Your thumbnail test



Go to my YouTube channel and pick one thumbnail I’ve already used.



Then:



recreate it in your own style

or improve it if you think it could be stronger



You are welcome to watermark your work if you’d like.



This will help me quickly see:



your taste

your design instincts

and how you think



3. Answer these questions in your application



Please answer:



What YouTube channels or creators do you think are edited/packaged really well?

Why do you think you’d be a good fit for this role?

What makes a YouTube video engaging in your opinion?



4. What softwares are you most comfortable using?



Final Note



I’m not looking for average.



I’m looking for someone who is:



talented

sharp

creative

detail-oriented

proactive

and genuinely excited about making great content



If that sounds like you, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Video Editor for High-Authority Short-Form Content (SMMA)
description:
We are looking for a video editor to edit short form social media videos for an authority-building brand. We focus on educational content, pain points, proof content, and personality content. Your job isn't just to cut clips; it's to maintain retention, tell a story and build trust through the edit.



$20.00/video. 7 videos/week to start.



We are seeking a long-term creative partner to grow with our agency. While we are starting with 30 videos per month, we are scaling quickly; for the right candidate, there is significant opportunity to increase your volume and take on a leadership role as our production needs expand.



What we're looking for:

- Mastery of "Pattern Interrupts" (zooms, graphics, motion graphics, b-roll, and text overlays that keep attention).

- Understanding of effective pacing for story telling, emphasis and retention

- Clean and purposeful aesthetic 

- Reliability (must meet all deadlines)



Requirements:

- Portfolio of short-form work (Reels/TikTok/Shorts).

- Ability to follow a detailed SOP.

- At least 2 years experience with short form social media video editing for brands 



Send your portfolio and answer these questions: 

- What is the most important element of a video's first 3 seconds to ensure someone doesn't scroll away?

- What video retention elements would you include when editing an "authority building" talking head style video.



To show us that you actually read this, please start your application with the word 'AUTHORITY' in the subject line.



We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Senior Webflow Developer
description:
About uBloom Digital



uBloom Digital is an Australian web design and brand development agency. We work with ambitious builders, architects, and property developers, helping them stand out, attract better clients, and grow through premium websites and sharp digital strategy.



Our work is held to a high standard. Every project we take on is built to make a genuine impact on our client's business, not just look good. We combine strong design, smart positioning, and technically polished execution to deliver websites that actually move the needle for the people we work with. Our team is everything to us and is what makes it all possible!



Job Details



As the Senior Webflow Developer, you will work closely with the CEO to lead the build and delivery of client projects from start to finish. You will take Figma designs, creative briefs, and strategic direction and turn them into premium, high-performing websites in Webflow.



This is not a support role. You will own the development side of every project, manage your own workload across multiple active builds, and be trusted to deliver without constant oversight. Over time, you will take on direct client communication and become a key part of how uBloom operates and grows.



This role suits someone who is highly skilled, self-directed, and genuinely proud of the work they put out. You care about the details, you communicate clearly, and you want to be part of something that is building toward something real.



uBloom is a growing creative and digital studio working with premium architecture, construction, and lifestyle brands. This role offers the opportunity to become deeply embedded in the business, develop strong development and project management skills, and take on increased responsibility over time. Prior experience is essential; a strong work ethic, sound judgement, and willingness to be part of the team are key.



Time requirement



9am - 6pm Brisbane Time (1 hour lunch)

Monday - Friday

13th Month Included

Australia Holiday Schedule



Responsibilities



Client project delivery



- Take completed Figma designs and build them in Webflow to a premium, pixel-accurate standard

- Develop custom interactions and animations using GSAP and native Webflow to a high level of polish

- Write clean JavaScript to extend functionality beyond native Webflow where required

- Work from partial briefs or design direction and apply enough design judgement to execute independently

- Manage multiple active projects simultaneously inside Notion without losing detail or missing deadlines

- Handle DNS configuration, domain setup, and full site launches independently



Existing project management



- Audit and clean up Webflow projects inherited from previous developers

- Restructure class names, organise styles, and implement cleaner development systems into existing builds

- Fix bugs, resolve layout and interaction issues, and improve overall project quality

- Implement website changes, new features, and additional functionality onto live client websites

- Navigate complex Webflow projects calmly and methodically without breaking what already works



Internal systems and frameworks



- Help build and refine uBloom's internal Webflow frameworks and component systems

- Contribute to the development of repeatable build processes that improve speed and consistency across projects

- Assist in building and improving internal task management systems inside Notion

- Document processes clearly so systems can be followed, improved, and handed over where needed



Standards and organisation



- Maintain clean, organised class naming conventions across every project without being asked

- Structure Webflow projects for long-term maintainability from the very first build session

- Communicate progress proactively, flag blockers early, and keep work moving without being chased

- Follow and actively contribute to uBloom's internal SOPs and build processes



Requirements for consideration



- 4+ years Webflow experience with a strong portfolio of premium, design-forward builds

- Advanced GSAP skills used regularly and confidently across real client projects

- Include an animal that best represents you in the subject line (required)

- Solid JavaScript ability for custom interactions, logic, and extended functionality

- Strong Figma-to-Webflow workflow with the design understanding to fill gaps independently

- Demonstrated ability to audit, clean up, and improve Webflow projects built by others

- A disciplined, consistent approach to class naming and project organisation

- Experience managing DNS settings and handling full site launches without support

- Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining a high standard of quality

Systems minded. You think about how to do things in a repeatable, scalable way, not just how to get the current task done

- Clear, professional written communication in English

- Reliable internet connection and consistent availability during agreed working hours

- Familiarity with Notion for project and task management

- Finsweet attributes experience is optional but a good advantage

- This must be your only role. Exclusive commitment is required



Why you’ll love working at uBloom Digital



This role goes well beyond executing tasks from a list. This is a fully remote role where you will be trusted with real responsibility from day one. You will be given genuine ownership over your work and the space to do it well. The work you build will be seen by hundreds of thousands of eyeballs across Australia and will directly shape how our high-end clients businesses grow and compete.



Culture matters at uBloom. We are a small, tight team that takes the work seriously but does not take itself too seriously. We care about quality, we move fast, and we back the people we work with. Outside of work the founders and team are into fitness, sport, health, and generally having a fun lifestyle, and we think that balance matters.



As the business grows, there is a real opportunity to grow with it. We value long-term relationships, reliability, and people who want to keep getting better. Initiative is rewarded with more responsibility, better projects, and progression over time. If you want to do some of the best work of your career inside a thoughtful and ambitious agency, this role is for you.



Compensation



$
USD per month 

13th Month Guaranteed



Overall, we are looking for a person with a positive attitude, professionalism, reliability, great time management skills, open communication, and a passion to learn! If this is you or someone you know, please apply with your resume and cover letter

title: Short-Form Video Editor – Social Media Agency
description:
I’m looking for a video editor to join NorthCut Media, a social media agency focused on organic growth for local businesses and personal brands.



To start, you’ll be editing simple talking head content — clean cuts, captions, pacing, basic graphics. As we build trust, the work expands into more graphic-intensive edits across multiple client accounts (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts).



This is a per-deliverable contract to start with a clear path to a monthly retainer for the right person.



What you’ll be editing

 - Talking head short-form clips (TikTok / Reels / Shorts)

 - Clean edits with captions, transitions, and on-screen text

 - Graphic-intensive content as volume grows

Compensation

 -  $15–$20 USD per reel used

 - Payment upon approval of each deliverable

 - Up to 2 revision rounds per video (feedback consolidated)

 - Path to monthly retainer based on performance and volume



What I’m looking for

 - Experience editing short-form content (portfolio required)

 - Understands pacing, retention, and what makes people watch

title: Outreach and Email Campaign Assistant
description:
About the Role:



We’re looking for a detail-oriented, proactive Outreach Assistant to help manage and execute cold
 campaigns for a U.S.-based credit card processing consultant. You’ll work directly with Sebastian, supporting his pipeline development efforts by identifying prospects, managing outreach lists, and executing campaigns that convert leads into scheduled calls.



Key Responsibilities:

-Build and manage cold
 lists for outreach campaigns.

-Write and send persuasive, professional
 sequences to book meetings with business owners.

-Monitor campaign performance (open rates, responses, conversion to meetings).

-Update and organize lead data in CRM (Pipedrive experience is a plus).

-Follow up with warm leads intelligently and professionally.

-Utilize AI tools to research prospects and improve campaign efficiency.



Required Skills and Experience

-Strong written and spoken English (C1 or higher).

-Proven experience in cold outreach, appointment setting, or
 marketing.

-Demonstrated ability to engage prospects and secure meetings via
.

-Strong understanding of sales communication principles: tone, timing, and personalization.

-Ability to work independently with strong attention to detail.

-(Preferred) Experience with Pipedrive CRM and basic
 automation tools

-(Bonus) Familiarity using AI tools to generate copy, segment leads, or analyze responses.



Working Hours: Flexible, but able to align with U.S. business hours as needed.



Skills Test: Reactivation



Objective:

Assess your ability to write a persuasive reactivation
 that re-engages small business owners who’ve previously spoken with Sebastian but haven’t scheduled a meeting yet.



Scenario:

Sebastian helps business owners save money and simplify their credit card processing and POS. Many of these owners met him briefly in person or were referred by mutual contacts but said it “wasn’t a good time” back then.



Sebastian’s Company’s Key Value Proposition is: Free equipment, no contracts, no cancellation fees, better rates, and best in class service.



Task:

Write a short, friendly reactivation
 (4-6 sentences) to an
 list of business owners, who have been contacted in person by Sebastian but have not converted to customers or been contacted since the initial in person meeting, encouraging them to reconnect and set up a call.



Your
 should:

-Sound genuine and personable, as if following up after meeting in person.

-Reference that they’ve spoken before, without sounding pushy or guilty.

-Emphasize benefits that matter to local business owners centered around Sebastian’s Key Value Proposition.

-End with a clear, low?pressure call?to?action to catch up or revisit their options.



Deliverables:

-Your sample reactivation
.

-A 2-3 sentence explanation of why you chose that tone and angle.

-A short note describing your experience with sales outreach, AI assisted writing tools, and CRM software.



In addition to the skills test please submit a current resume or CV

title: General Accountant
description:
About Us

We are a Global Automotive Accessory business committed to excellence and growth. We are looking for a dedicated and skilled accountant/bookkeeper to join our team long-term as a trusted advisor and partner. This is a full-time, remote position with significant opportunity for professional growth as our business expands.



The Role: Your Opportunity to Shine

We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Accountant/Bookkeeper to take full ownership of our financial management. You will be instrumental in ensuring the accuracy and efficiency of our accounting operations.



Key Responsibilities:

- Manage Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR).

- Handle Payroll processing.

- Perform comprehensive Bank and Supplier Account Reconciliations.

- Prepare accurate Monthly Financial Reports.

- Generate various Ad Hoc Financial Reports as required.

- Ensure timely submission of statutory filings (e.g., BAS - training provided on jurisdiction-specific requirements).

- Actively seek, recommend, and implement process improvements within the accounting function to enhance efficiency and accuracy.

• Be flexible and available for out of hours meetings with our global offices

• Ability to travel to Sydney Australia for Training – 2 weeks initial training in Sydney



What We're Looking For: Your Skills

- Proven experience as an Accountant or Bookkeeper (5+ years).

- Highly Experienced with Multiple Accounting Systems: While we use a custom in-house system (training provided), a background successfully navigating and utilizing different accounting software is highly valued.

- Google Workspace Expert (REQUIRED):

- Strong proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs).

- Advanced Google Sheets Skills is a MUST. You must be comfortable and highly skilled with complex formulas (e.g., ARRAYFORMULA, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, QUERY, conditional formatting, Pivot Tables).

- Process Improvement Mindset: You are not just looking to execute tasks, but to find better, faster, and more efficient ways of working.

- Long-Term Commitment: We are seeking a reliable, dedicated individual looking for a stable, long-term working relationship.



Work Hours

Standard Monday to Friday, 8 hours per day 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM AEST and must be available for global meetings outside these hours and emergencies.



Join Our Team?

Stability & Growth: This is a long-term position with a growing global business.

Career Development: There is a clear pathway for the right person to take on more responsibility, oversee new tasks, and potentially supervise others as the company expands.

Impact: Your work will directly contribute to the company's financial health and strategic decision-making.







How to Apply

If you are a highly skilled Accountant with a passion for process improvement and advanced Google Sheets mastery, we want to hear from you!

To be considered, please reply to this post with the following:

- Your resume detailing your relevant accounting experience.

- A brief cover letter explaining why you are the best fit for this role.

- MANDATORY: In your application, please describe a complex Google Sheets formula you have recently used, and explain what problem it solved for you.



Salary:

We offer a competitive market-rate salary that will be commensurate with your experience and skills.



We look forward to finding our next long-term partner!

title: AI Fashion Product Lister | E-commerce (Google Ads Optimised)
description:
Who We Are:

We are a growing fashion e-commerce brand looking for a detail-oriented AI Fashion Product Lister to create high-quality, Google Ads optimised product listings. You'll be responsible for product photography direction, image editing, SEO-optimised titles, and compelling product descriptions.



What You'll Do



Create complete, Google Ads optimised product listings

Ensure all product images meet our quality standards (see below)

Write SEO-optimised product titles using provided keywords

Write clear, engaging product descriptions that convert

Use AI tools to assist with content creation and image editing

Work with our guidelines document to maintain consistency across all listings





Product Image Requirements

This is the most important part of the role. Every listing must include the following images:

Front Image (Required)



Clean chest shot from the front — close-up of the product

Product must fill 50–75% of the image frame

White or neutral background, no distractions

Key features and details clearly visible

Minimum resolution: 2048 x 2048 pixels

Proper lighting, no harsh shadows



Full Body Shot (Required)



Front view, head to toe

Same background and lighting standards as above



Back Image (Required)



Clear photograph of the product from the back

Same background style and lighting as the front image

Show design details, labels, tags, or sizing information



Product Detail Shots (Required — 2 to 3 images)



Close-up shots of the product without the model

Show fabric texture, stitching, labels, zippers, buttons, or any unique details

Same quality and lighting standards



Color Variations



Each color variant must have its own front close-up image

Main color can have additional angles (recommended)

Additional angles for other color variants are optional





Product Title Requirements



Use the exact keywords we provide for each product

Follow this structure: [Product Type] + [Key Feature] + [Size/Color]

Keep titles under 150 characters

Most important keywords go first

NO all caps, NO promotional language ("Best Seller", "Free Shipping")





Product Description Requirements



150–300 words per product

Concise, informative, and engaging

Include: opening line, 3–5 key features, material/specs, use case, call to action

Use provided keywords naturally — no keyword stuffing

Write for humans first, SEO second





Required Skills & Experience



Can list 5-10 products by these requirements

Minimum 1 year experience in e-commerce product listing

Experience with fashion/apparel listings is a strong plus

Familiarity with Google Ads / Google Shopping optimisation

Proficient in SEO tools (SEMrush, Ahrefs, Google Search Console, or similar)

Experience with AI tools (Nano banana, Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney, Dall-E, or similar)

Proficient in image editing (Canva or similar)

Strong written English

Extreme attention to detail

Experience with the ARTlist platform is a plus





How to Apply

To be considered, please include ALL of the following in your application:



A brief introduction explaining why you're a great fit for this role

A link to your portfolio or samples of previous e-commerce product listings

Your experience with Google Ads optimised listings specifically

Whether you have experience with the ARTlist platform

A link to your CV / resume (Google Docs link is fine)



?? Important: Applications that do not include items 1–5 above will not be reviewed. Please start your application with the words "FASHION READY" so I know you've read this entire post.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Arrange songs to maintain listener retention and flow

Create a cohesive listening experience (study, focus, relaxation)

Think in terms of session time, not just individual tracks

YOU MUST INCLUDE IN YOUR APPLICATION:

Applications without this info will not be considered.

Has real experience with YouTube music channels

Understands what keeps people listening for long periods

Can think strategically (not just execute tasks)

Has a good sense of music flow, mood, and transitions



Details:



Part-time / hourly role

Ongoing work if it’s a good fit



To Apply:

Start your application with: “PLAYLIST FLOW”

Then include the required info above.

title: AI-Powered Resume & Content VA
description:
The Opportunity

You will support the business owner directly, handling the tasks that keep the practice running day to day. This is not a traditional admin role. The owner uses custom ChatGPT prompts, Loom video training, and Google Docs to document every workflow so you are never left guessing. Execution, consistency, and proactive communication are what matter most here.

title: Real Estate HDR Photo Editor
description:
About the Role

Marked Homes is a professional real estate media company serving agents and property owners across the US. We produce HDR photography, video walkthroughs, drone footage, and 360 virtual tours for residential and luxury listings in the Branson / Table Rock Lake market and beyond.

We need a skilled real estate photo editor who specializes in manual HDR hand blending in Lightroom. You will receive raw bracketed exposures via Google Drive and return polished, professional JPEGs ready for MLS delivery — consistently, on time, every batch.

This is a long-term ongoing role with growing volume. Great editors never run out of work here.



Hard Requirements

If you cannot meet all of the following, do not apply:



Minimum 2 years of real estate HDR photo editing experience

Manual hand blending technique — no automated HDR merge

24-hour turnaround on all batches. No exceptions.

Reliable internet connection for Google Drive transfers

Available to receive and deliver files 6 days a week





What You Will Do

Every shoot you receive a Google Drive folder of raw bracketed exposures — typically 3 to 5 exposures per shot. You manually blend these in Lightroom into a single finished image per scene.

Core workflow:



Manual HDR hand blending in Adobe Lightroom

Window pull / exposure masking — exterior windows must show the view, not blow out

Interior exposure balancing — ceilings, floors, fixtures feel naturally lit

White balance and color correction — neutral, consistent across every image in the set

Sky replacement or cleanup — natural only, no dramatic oversaturation

Perspective and lens correction — straight verticals, no keystoning

Basic cleanup — cords, small distractions removed where appropriate

Final export — JPEGs organized by scene, uploaded to shared Drive output folder





What Finished Photos Must Look Like



Bright, clean, inviting — not dark, not overprocessed

Natural light feel — not tone-mapped or HDR-heavy

Windows show the exterior without overexposure

Consistent color temperature across the entire set

Straight verticals throughout

No halos, banding, or exposure artifacts

Every image looks like it belongs to the same shoot





File Workflow



You receive Google Drive link with raw bracketed exposures

Download, hand blend, export final JPEGs

Upload finished files to designated output folder in same Drive

Notify via WhatsApp when batch is complete

We review and request revisions if needed

Payment sent weekly via Wise or PayPal





Compensation



Rate: $0.10–$0.25 per finished photo (set based on test results)

Typical batch: 40–120 photos per shoot

Volume: Multiple shoots per week, growing

Payment: Weekly via Wise or PayPal



Example monthly earnings at volume:



100 
 = $240/month

300 
 = $720/month

500 
 = $1,600/month



Volume grows as we expand. Top editors get priority assignment.



To Apply

You will be asked for:



Portfolio link — before/after real estate HDR edits required

Your per-photo rate

Turnaround time for a 100-photo batch

Software stack

Timezone and availability

Window blending technique question — required, no skipping



Shortlisted applicants receive a paid 10-photo test batch at their quoted rate.

We are not looking for the cheapest editor. We are looking for the most consistent one.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Attention, Rockstars! We’re looking for a full-time Executive Assistant. This role provides high-level administrative support to the CEO, ensuring the smooth operation of daily activities. This role involves managing schedules, coordinating meetings, handling communications, and performing a wide range of administrative tasks to support executives in their strategic responsibilities. The Executive Assistant acts as a key point of contact and is responsible for maintaining confidentiality, ensuring efficient workflow, and managing critical tasks with professionalism and discretion.



Responsibilities:

Manage the executive’s calendar, scheduling meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements, and ensuring that time is allocated efficiently.

Act as the primary point of contact for internal and external communications, screening calls,
s, and visitors, and directing inquiries as appropriate.

Coordinate meetings, conferences, and events, including preparing agendas, arranging logistics, and ensuring all materials are available.

Prepare and edit correspondence, reports, presentations, and other documents on behalf of the executive.

Handle confidential information with the utmost discretion and ensure sensitive documents are securely maintained.

Assist with special projects, conducting research, gathering data, and preparing reports as required by the executive.

Liaise with other departments and executives to ensure smooth communication and collaboration on cross-functional initiatives.

Anticipate the executive's needs and provide support to ensure they are well-prepared for meetings, presentations, and other engagements.



Qualifications/Requirements:

Proven experience (1-2 years) as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or in a similar role supporting senior executives.

Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to prioritize tasks, manage multiple responsibilities, and meet deadlines.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a professional and polished demeanor.

Strong attention to detail and accuracy in completing tasks.

High level of discretion and confidentiality when handling sensitive information.

Proficiency in GSuite (Doc, Sheet, Slides, Drive) and Zoom Workspace and familiarity with office management tools and software.

Ability to work independently and proactively, with a strong sense of initiative.

Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with executives, staff, and external contacts.



Our Purpose:

To develop and market products that help individuals to grow in multiple facets in life.

To Increase awareness and skill sets for the public we serve.

To make a significant difference in the quality of life of people not reaching their potential and those often left behind.

To produce and develop Millionaire closers.



By applying, you will show that you are committed to being a SUCCESS, and your success is important to us. I believe in supporting our clients and helping associates achieve their financial goals.



The position is Full-Time (remote) between the hours of 8:00AM - 5:00PM Pacific Standard Time, Monday through Friday.

THE OPPORTUNITY:

Every successful introduction that leads to partnership earns you serious money. One good partnership could net you $5,000-10,000 in your first month.





COMPENSATION:

Monetize your network through three massive income streams: guaranteed ETH salary, instant token unlocks, and recurring commissions. We pay you to open doors you’ve already built, while we handle 100% of the closing.



Guaranteed Base Pay: You will receive $10/hour (capped at 10 hours/week),       for simply activating your network. 50% paid in Ethereum (ETH) for immediate cash and 50% in $GMI tokens, which are subject to an 18-month vesting schedule, building a long-term position in the $GMI mutli-chain ecosystem.



Performance Bonus: Every 5 successful partnership you close triggers an immediate unlock of 20% your vested tokens. This allows you to bypass the 18-month vesting period and access your earnings as soon as you deliver results.

Note: A "successful partnership" is defined by the project signing the LP Bonds integration agreement and going live on our marketplace.



Additional Commission: 

- 25% commission on ALL upgrade fees from your partnerships

- Level 2 upgrades: $40 upgrade fee, you get $10 each

- Level 3 upgrades: $80 upgrade fee, you get $20 each

- Level 4 upgrades: $160 upgrade fee, you get $40 each

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

- Hunger to earn based on results, not hours





YOU DON’T NEED:

- To close deals (I handle that)

THE ROLE:

Simply introduce me to decision-makers at crypto projects. I close the deals, you collect commissions. Every user from your partnerships who upgrades their bonds puts money in your pocket..

Example: ONE medium-sized launchpad partnership generating 100 L4 bonds = $8,000 for you.

- Your
 handle

title: Essay Editor/Academic Writer
description:
We’re an academic consulting firm that helps high school students gain admission to top-tier universities in the U.S., and we’re currently seeking a part-time Essay Editor/Academic Writer with strong writing, editing, and critical thinking skills to join our team.



This role is ideal for someone who can take rough or underdeveloped student essays and turn them into polished, well-structured pieces that read naturally and professionally. The position is part-time and flexible, but it’s also deadline-driven and requires strong reliability, responsiveness, and attention to detail.



This is not a general content writing role. We’re specifically looking for someone who can revise and rewrite essays so they are clearer, stronger, and more compelling while still aligning with the original prompt, intended message, and required word count.



Key Responsibilities:

• Edit and rewrite high school-level essays based on specific prompts and word count requirements, improving structure, clarity, grammar, tone, and overall impact.

• Manage multiple essay assignments at once while meeting tight turnaround times, typically within 24–48 hours, and communicate proactively regarding deadlines or issues that may affect delivery.



Applicants should be able to:

• Rewrite and strengthen weak or underdeveloped drafts without losing the original intent, while elevating flow, structure, readability, and overall quality.

• Demonstrate excellent grammar, sentence control, and word choice, and write with a level of polish that reads as fluent and natural in English.



To proceed with your application, please send a message with your resume, 2-3 writing samples for review, and an estimate of how many hours you can commit each week.

title: Graphic Design & AI Creative Production
description:
Position: Virtual Assistant — Graphic Design & AI Creative Production

iMedia Exposure | Remote | Part-Time to Full-Time



About iMedia Exposure

iMedia Exposure is a boutique brand strategy and marketing agency founded on the belief that aligned brands build lasting legacies. We serve soul-led entrepreneurs, wellness leaders, coaches, and visionary founders — guiding them from clarity to visibility through elevated brand ecosystems, strategic marketing systems, and intentional design. Every asset we produce carries a standard of excellence that reflects the caliber of our clients and the integrity of our work.



The Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented, creatively driven Virtual Assistant specializing in graphic design and AI-assisted creative production to support our growing client portfolio. This is not a template-and-output role. We are looking for someone who thinks visually, works with precision, and understands that great design tells a story before a single word is read.

You will be responsible for producing website graphics, social media content, and print-ready materials across multiple client brands — each with its own aesthetic, audience, and strategic intention. You must be fluent in both traditional design tools and emerging AI creative platforms, and you must know when to use each one effectively.



What You Will Do



Design and produce on-brand graphics for client websites, including banners, section headers, feature images, and lead magnet visuals

Create cohesive social media graphics including static posts, carousel slides, story templates, reel covers, and branded overlay elements for Instagram, LinkedIn, and

Develop print-ready materials including flyers, event programs, one-sheets, sell sheets, and promotional collateral

Utilize AI image generation tools (such as Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, or similar platforms) to ideate, prototype, and produce original creative assets

Maintain and apply client brand guidelines with precision — color, typography, tone, and visual language must be consistent across every deliverable

Organize and manage a clean, structured creative asset library within our project management and file storage systems

Communicate proactively about timelines, revisions, and creative direction

Adapt quickly when priorities shift without sacrificing quality





What We Are Looking For



Demonstrated experience in graphic design for digital and print — a portfolio is required

Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign) and Canva Pro at a professional level

Working knowledge of AI creative tools and a clear understanding of how to prompt effectively for brand-aligned results

A strong aesthetic sensibility — you can look at a brand and immediately understand its visual language

Exceptional attention to detail; you notice when something is off by two pixels and it bothers you

The ability to manage multiple client projects simultaneously without losing quality or cohesion

Clear written communication and the ability to receive feedback professionally and efficiently

Familiarity with project management tools such as Asana, Notion, or similar platforms

Experience working within brand guidelines for premium, service-based, or wellness-adjacent brands is a strong advantage





Our Aesthetic Standard

Our agency works within a refined, elevated visual space. Our clients are established leaders — their brand presence must reflect authority, warmth, and intentionality. We do not produce trend-chasing content. We build visual identities and marketing assets that are timeless in feel, strategic in function, and distinctive in character. If your design instinct leads you toward clean structure, purposeful color, and meaningful composition — you will fit here.



How to Apply

Please submit the following:



Your portfolio or a curated selection of relevant work (website graphics, social media assets, and print materials preferred)

A brief introduction — tell us who you are as a designer, what tools you work with most fluently, and how you approach a new brand project

Your availability and hourly rate or project-based pricing structure

title: FOIA Bodycam Video Requester & Researcher YouTube Channel — True Crime, Police Accountability, Legal Commentary & News
description:
About the channel

We run a YouTube channel covering police accountability and oversight, true crime, legal commentary, and general news and current events. Our content is built on real footage and public records — bodycam videos, dashcam clips, i
 reports, and court documents obtained through FOIA and state public records requests across the U.S.



We need an experienced researcher who has actually done this before — someone who knows how to navigate agency portals, craft effective request letters, handle delays and denials, and get results.



What you'll do



Research newsworthy i
 using U.S. news sources, court records, social media, and public databases

Identify the correct law enforcement agencies or departments holding relevant bodycam footage or records

Draft and submit FOIA / state public records requests — tailored to each state's specific laws and deadlines

Follow up persistently with agencies, escalate delays, and file appeals when requests are improperly denied

Track all open requests, statuses, and received materials in a shared Google Sheets log

Summarize i
 background and flag high-priority footage for the channel team

Source supporting materials — court filings, civil suit documents, police reports — for legal commentary videos

Use platforms like MuckRock, PACER, and CourtListener as part of your standard workflow

Requirements — please read carefully



Must have prior experience submitting U.S. FOIA or state public records requests — this is non-negotiable

Proven ability to draft clear, properly formatted public records request letters

Familiarity with how to handle denials, exemptions, and fee waivers

Knowledge of state-by-state public records laws and response timelines

Excellent English writing skills — formal, professional, and precise

Strong online research skills using U.S. government portals, court databases, and news archives

Highly organized — managing multiple open requests across different agencies at once

Self-directed with no need for hand-holding — you know the process and can run it independently

Reliable internet connection and responsive async communication (Slack,
, or similar)

Strongly preferred



Experience with MuckRock, PACER, CourtListener, or similar platforms

Background in journalism, paralegal work, investigative research, or legal studies

Experience appealing FOIA denials or navigating Vaughn indices

Familiarity with U.S. civil rights law, use-of-force standards, or criminal procedure

Prior work supporting a media outlet, YouTube channel, podcast, or publication

Project structure & pay



Work is assigned on a per-project basis. Each project is tied to a specific video or story and may involve one or more FOIA requests. You'll be paid hourly ($5–$8/hr based on experience and track record) for verified hours per project. Strong performers will receive consistent ongoing work as new content is produced.



How to apply



Send a cover letter (4–6 sentences) that specifically describes your FOIA or public records experience. Tell us what types of requests you've submitted, which agencies or states you've worked with, and what tools you use. Generic applications will not be considered.



If you have examples of requests you've submitted, agencies you've successfully obtained records from, or any related work samples, please include them.



Prior FOIA or U.S. public records request experience is required for this role. Please do not apply if you have never submitted a public records request before.



To confirm you read this post in full, include the phrase "public records matter" in your cover letter. Applications without it will be automatically disqualified.

title: Operations VA / Order Fulfillment & Customer Service Specialist
description:
I am hiring a highly organized, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant based in the Philippines to manage the day-to-day operations of an eBay store. This role is for someone who can take ownership of order processing, customer communication, issue resolution, and general store support with minimal supervision.



The ideal candidate has experience with eBay, ecommerce operations, order fulfillment workflows, customer service, and supplier coordination. I am looking for someone dependable who can help keep the business running smoothly and proactively solve problems before they escalate.



Main Responsibilities:



Order Fulfillment



Monitor eBay for new orders throughout the day

Process and fulfill customer orders accurately and quickly using approved supplier workflows

Double-check shipping addresses, item details, delivery speeds, and customer notes before placing any order

Upload and confirm tracking information in eBay

Monitor shipment progress and identify delayed, canceled, or problematic orders

Follow up on supplier issues and work to prevent late shipment defects

Maintain accurate fulfillment logs and order status updates



Customer Service



Respond to customer messages professionally and quickly

Handle pre-sale questions about products, shipping times, item availability, and returns

Resolve post-sale issues such as delayed shipments, damaged items, tracking problems, refunds, cancellations, and returns

Manage return requests and refund cases according to store policies

Help maintain strong account health by reducing negative feedback and defects

Escalate sensitive cases only when necessary, with a summary and recommended solution



Daily Store Operations



Review open orders, messages, returns, and cases each day

Flag orders with address issues, stock issues, pricing changes, or fulfillment risks

Monitor inventory availability and communicate when listings may need updating

Watch for supplier-side problems that could affect fulfillment performance

Keep internal SOPs updated as workflows improve

Provide a daily summary of completed tasks, open issues, and priorities for the next day



Account Support / Administrative Tasks



Track key metrics such as orders fulfilled, late shipments, cancellations, refunds, and customer response times

Maintain spreadsheets or dashboards for order tracking and issue management

Help document repeat customer issues and recommend process improvements

Assist with basic listing updates, title edits, or inventory adjustments if needed

Help keep operations compliant with marketplace policies and internal business rules



What I Need From You



Prior experience with eBay store operations or ecommerce order management

Strong written English and professional customer service skills

Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow SOPs exactly

Ability to work independently and make sound judgment calls

Fast response time and strong sense of urgency

Reliable internet and availability during agreed working hours

Experience with spreadsheets, order trackers, and ecommerce tools

Ability to provide clear daily updates without being micromanaged

title: Dental Reimbursement Specialist - Position No. DRS265
description:
Dental Reimbursement Specialist 



Dental Billing Innovate is seeking a detail-oriented Dental Billing Insurance Collector Specialist to support end-to-end dental revenue cycle and insurance collection operations. The specialist will be responsible for submitting and monitoring insurance claims through dental practice management systems such as Dentrix, Open Dental, EagleSoft, and other platforms, ensuring accurate coding, timely filing, and proper follow-up on unpaid or underpaid claims.



This role includes managing aging reports, resolving claim discrepancies with insurance carriers, initiating appeals when necessary, uploading explanation of benefits (EOBs), and maintaining clear, auditable documentation of all account activities. The specialist will communicate regularly with client offices and LSI management regarding claim status, respond promptly to insurance-related inquiries, and escalate operational issues as needed.



The position also involves participating in monthly performance meetings to review production results and provide recommendations to improve insurance collection processes and overall revenue cycle performance.

title: Virtual Assistant Manager
description:
Part Time (Remote, Philippines hours)

Schedule: 5 hours/day, 5 days/week — Tuesday through Saturday. Work window 6:00 AM – 1:00 PM PHT; schedule 5 paid working hours within that window (two unpaid breaks included: Lunch for 1 hour and 30 minutes and one 30 minute break). Part time to start with a clear path to full time as revenue and performance permit.



About Us

We are a growing finance blog covering investing, real estate investing, business acquisitions, budgeting, and personal finance. Primary revenue today is Amazon affiliates and display ads. We are expanding revenue streams and may add a clothing brand in the future. This role is operationally critical: you will run content operations, maintain quality control, create and maintain SOPs, and keep the publishing machine running smoothly. Hiring and candidate selection remain the owner’s responsibility.



Role Summary

You will manage day?to?day content operations and the existing content team (writers, SMM, contractors) once they are assigned. Your responsibilities include owning the publishing calendar, QA and publishing to WordPress, creating and maintaining SOPs, training and coaching tea
mbers on processes (not recruiting), and delivering regular operational reports to the owner. You will free the owner to focus on strategy and revenue growth.

Key Responsibilities

    • Manage daily content operations and coordinate work across writers and contractors assigned by the owner. 

    • Maintain and enforce the editorial and publishing calendar; assign and track tasks in the project management tool. 

    • Review drafts for formatting, affiliate link accuracy, image sourcing, and basic SEO checklist items. 

    • Publish posts to WordPress (editor role) and perform post?publish QA and fixes. 

    • Create, maintain, and improve SOPs for publishing, affiliate link insertion, image sourcing, editing, and content QA. 

    • Train and coach writers and contractors on SOPs and publishing standards (training existing hires; owner handles hiring). 

    • Track and report KPIs: publishing adherence, error rates, time from draft to publish, and quality metrics. 

    • Provide daily asynchronous updates and a weekly written operations report to the owner. 



Must Have

    • 1+ years remote VA or operations experience, with at least 4 months managing or coaching remote contractors or content teams. 

    • Strong written English and professional communication. 

    • Proven process orientation and experience creating SOPs and checklists. 

    • Reliable internet and quiet workspace; ability to work the required PHT hours. 

    • High attention to detail, especially with affiliate links, formatting, and publishing accuracy. 

Nice to Have

    • Experience with affiliate sites or Amazon Associates. 

    • Basic SEO knowledge and familiarity with Yoast or Rank Math. 

    • Prior experience working with Filipino writers or remote content teams for blogs. 

    • Comfortable with WordPress, Google Workspace, project management tool (Asana, Trello, ClickUp).

    • Knowledge and experience using 
 is a major plus.

Compensation

    • $4 – $5 per hour depending on experience and demonstrated skills. 

    • Paid trial task (3–6 hours) required before hire. 

    • 2?week paid trial period followed by a 30?day probation. Clear path to full time and raises tied to KPIs and revenue milestones. 



Unpaid Trial Task (5–10 hours) if more than 5 hours it will be split for the next day.

Deliverables: ( will only be used for screening purposes. )

1.  A simple content calendar (2 weeks)

 Tasks assigned to roles (you, writers, SMM)

 Deadlines and dependencies

A brief explanation of their workflow

If selected you will be asked to complete a Paid trial Task as described below.



Paid Trial Task (5–10 hours) if more than 5 hours it will be split for the next day.

Deliverables:

    1. One?page Onboarding Checklist for a new part?time writer (focus on access, SOP links, formatting rules, affiliate link policy, and first?week tasks). 

    2. Publishing QA Exercise: Take a provided draft (owner will supply) and produce a publish?ready WordPress checklist and a list of corrections/edits you would make before publishing. 

    3. SOP Samples: Draft a concise 3–5 step SOP for inserting and verifying Amazon affiliate links on a post. Draft a concise 5-7 step SOP for writers to follow for article creation. 

Evaluation Criteria

    • Clarity and usability of deliverables 30% 

    • Process thinking and structure 30% 

    • Written communication quality 20% 

    • Practical completeness and attention to affiliate details 20% 

Hiring Process

    1. Submit application with required items below. 

    2. Shortlist and written screening. 

    3. Paid trial task (5-10 hours). 

    4. 2?week paid trial period. 

    5. 60?day probation with KPIs and regular check?ins. 

KPIs You Will Be Measured On

    • Publishing schedule adherence. 

    • Error rate in published posts (formatting, affiliate links, article quality). 

    • Average time from draft to publish. 

    • Completion and quality of SOPs and training materials. 

    • Timely daily updates and weekly reports. 

How to Apply Please apply through this platform and include:

    1. Resume or CV. 

    2. Two?minute video introduction (recorded on your phone is fine). 

    3. Short answers to these screening questions: 

        ? Describe your experience managing or coaching remote content teams and one measurable outcome. 

        ? Can you reliably work the PHT hours listed? 

        ? Share a link or paste an example of an SOP you created. 

        ? Why are you interested in our finance content operations? 

    4. Availability to start and expected hourly rate within the posted range. 

Red Flags

    • Poor written English or unclear trial deliverables. 

    • Inability to commit to the PHT schedule. 

    • No examples of process documentation or experience managing/coaching remote teams. 

    • Vague answers about past results or lack of measurable outcomes. 



Final Notes This is a high?impact role focused on operational excellence, quality control, and process ownership. The right candidate will be given responsibility to run day?to?day content operations, improve workflows, and coach the team. Growth opportunities and a path to full time are available for strong performers.

title: Claude AI Automation Specialist
description:
Claude AI & Process Automation Specialist



Company: Pinnacle Logistics Group (Phoenix, AZ - Remote/Philippines-based)

Type: Full-Time

Benefits: 13th Month Pay, Paid Time Off, Claude Enterprise Account (unlimited tokens)

Salary Range: 55,000 - 82,000 PHP /month ($1,000 - $1,500 USD / mo) plus 13th month pay



READ THE WHOLE POSTING - THOSE WHO DON'T FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED



ABOUT US



Pinnacle Logistics Group is a fast-growing, tech-enabled 3PL (third-party logistics) company. We move freight across the US and Mexico -- full truckload, LTL, drayage, and ocean. We're a 46-person team with 35 tea
mbers based in the Philippines. We've built a reputation for using AI and automation to punch way above our weight class, and we're doubling down on that advantage.





THE ROLE



This is not a Zapier jockey position. We need someone who lives inside Claude and understands how to use it as a platform -- not just a chatbot.



You'll be embedded with our team, learning how every role works day to day, and then building AI-powered automations that eliminate hours of manual work every week. The difference between this role and a generic "automation specialist" is that Claude is the backbone of our tech stack. We use Claude Enterprise, Claude Code, Claude Projects, the Claude API, MCP servers, and Claude's computer use capabilities. We need someone who already understands this ecosystem or can get up to speed fast.



The right person sees a teammate copy-pasting between three tabs and thinks: "I can build a Claude workflow that does that in zero steps." You'll own the full cycle -- observe the process, design the solution, build it, test it, and hand it off.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



- Shadowing tea
mbers across operations, carrier sales, and account management to map their daily workflows

- Identifying manual, repetitive tasks and building Claude-powered automations that eliminate them

- Building with Claude Code -- writing and shipping automation scripts, API integrations, and internal tools using Claude as your primary coding environment

- Designing and maintaining Claude Projects with custom system prompts and knowledge bases for different team functions

- Connecting Claude to our stack via MCP servers and the Claude API (Gmail, Google Drive, Google Chat, Close CRM, Airtable, Front, Fireflies)

- Building integration pipelines using Zapier, 
, Python/Flask, and webhooks -- with Claude doing the heavy lifting on logic and code generation

- Parsing
s, syncing data between systems, auto-posting to channels, and building AI-powered triage workflows

- Staying current on Claude product updates, new features, and capabilities -- then figuring out how we can use them before anyone else does

- Documenting what you build and training tea
mbers on how to use the tools you create

- Reporting on time saved, processes improved, and automation ROI





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Claude & AI (Required)

- Deep, hands-on experience with Claude (Anthropic) -- not just casual chatbot usage, but real workflow building

- Experience using Claude Code or similar AI coding assistants to build and ship working tools

- Familiarity with the Claude API, system prompts, and how to structure effective prompts for complex tasks

- Understanding of how to use Claude Projects to create role-specific AI assistants with custom instructions and knowledge

- Awareness of Claude's current capabilities and limitations -- you follow product updates and know what's new



Automation & Technical (Required)

- Working experience with at least two of: Zapier, 
, Python, APIs/webhooks

- Can read API documentation and build integrations independently

- Basic scripting ability (Python preferred, JavaScript/Node.js also fine)

- Comfortable with Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Chat, Drive)



Soft Skills (Required)

- Strong observation skills -- you need to understand how people actually work before you can improve anything

- Self-starter mentality. We'll give you priorities, but you figure out the "how" on your own. Google it, ask Claude, read the docs, watch a tutorial -- we don't care how you learn, just that you deliver.

- Clear written and verbal English -- you'll work directly with company leadership daily



Bonus Points

- Experience with MCP servers or building custom Claude integrations

- Familiarity with Airtable, Front, 
, or Fireflies.ai

- Background in logistics, freight, or supply chain

- Experience with Flask or any lightweight web framework

- You've built a Claude Project, custom GPT, or AI agent that you can show us





WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT



You won't be dragging and dropping Zapier nodes all day. You'll be working directly with company leadership on a live backlog of automation projects that have real, measurable impact. We already have the ideas. We already have Claude Enterprise with unlimited tokens. We already have the integrations mapped out. What we need is someone who can execute -- fast, clean, and independently.



We're building toward a future where every role at Pinnacle has AI woven into their daily workflow. You'll be the person who makes that happen.



A word of honesty: if you need hand-holding or step-by-step instructions to get started, this isn't the right fit. We value people who see a broken process and can't help but fix it.





HOW TO APPLY 

(THESE ARE REQUIRED - IF NOT INCLUDED THEN YOUR APPLICATION WILL BE IGNORED)



Send us:



- A video message introducing yourself and what experience qualifies you for the role

- Examples of what you've built with Claude or other AI tools (screenshots, videos, links, or descriptions)

- Examples of automations or integrations you've built (any platform)

- Your expected monthly salary in PHP

Compensation:

-$4–$5 per hour

-Performance bonuses per qualified lead and closed deal



Schedule:

-20–40 hours per week

-Must overlap with U.S. Eastern Time (EST)



KPIs (Success Metrics):

-50–100 calls per day

-5–10 leads per day

-Consistent follow-up activity



Performance Expectations:

This is a results-driven role. You will be expected to meet daily calling and follow-up targets.



Growth Opportunity:

This is a long-term position with opportunity to grow into a higher role (Acquisitions / Sales).



Trial Period:

All candidates will go through a 3–5 day paid trial period before full hiring.



To Apply:

-Short voice recording (30–60 seconds introducing yourself)

-Brief description of your experience with cold calling

-Confirm you are comfortable making 50–100 calls/day



About Us:

We are focused on finding discounted real estate deals (distressed properties, off-market opportunities). We value consistency, communication, and long-term teamwork.

title: Marketing Specialist (Execution-Focused)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



We’re hiring a Marketing Specialist to own the execution of marketing for our fitness business.

This role exists to ensure marketing initiatives are executed consistently and effectively across content creation, video editing, social media,
 marketing, website updates, paid digital campaigns, and outreach. Strategy and priorities are clearly defined. This position owns implementation, follow-through, and quality control across channels.



The day-to-day work of this role is centered around content creation, video editing, and social execution, while also requiring strong competency in paid digital advertising and CRM campaign execution.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Marketing Execution & Calendar Ownership



• Execute defined marketing initiatives across video, social media,
, website, print, paid digital campaigns, and outreach

• Own day-to-day implementation of the marketing calendar

• Coordinate timing, sequencing, and delivery across channels

• Ensure initiatives are launched on time and on brand



2. Content & Video Production

• Edit and publish video content 

• Create and schedule social media content (copy + visual) across platforms

• Repurpose content for social media, YouTube,
, paid ads, and website updates

• Create supporting graphic design assets for both digital and print, including social graphics, flyers, posters, postcards, and campaign visuals for promotions and events.

• Maintain a consistent weekly content publishing cadence



3. Paid Digital Advertising (Meta Platforms)

• Execute and optimize Meta paid advertising 
 & Instagram)

• Manage targeting, creative variations, and budgets

• Leverage boosted posts, stories, and ads effectively

• Monitor performance and optimize based on results

• Ensure paid ads integrate with CRM and follow-up systems

 

4.
 Marketing & CRM Execution (GoHighLevel)

• Execute
 and CRM campaigns focused on lead follow-up, referral initiatives, reactivation of former clients, and program awareness across adult, youth, and family offerings

• Build, manage, and maintain automated campaign workflows and sequences inside GoHighLevel CRM

• Ensure accurate tagging, automation logic, sequencing, and delivery across all campaigns

• Execute outbound
 outreach campaigns supporting speaking engagements, partnership development, and internship



5. Website, Landing Pages & SEO (Squarespace)

• Independently manage Squarespace websites

• Build and publish landing pages supporting campaigns and client acquisition

• Ensure pages are clean, functional, and mobile-optimized

• Maintain on-page SEO fundamentals including titles, meta descriptions, headers and alt text



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

• Strong interest in fitness, performance, or health preferred

• Hands-on experience running and optimizing Meta paid ads

• CRM campaign execution experience (GoHighLevel preferred)

• Proficiency in Squarespace

• SEO fundamentals

• Comfortable working independently with video editing and content publishing tools

• Strong
 writing skills

• Basic graphic design ability

• Working knowledge of AI editing & content creation tools



WHAT THIS ROLE REQUIRES

• Strong execution skills and attention to detail

• Ability to manage multiple initiatives without dropping deadlines

• Comfort working independently within defined priorities

• Consistent follow-through and accountability



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

• A marketing strategy or brand positioning role

• A creative director or design-only role

• A sales or closing role



HOW SUCCESS IS MEASURED

• New clients attributed to marketing

• Videos edited and published

• Game Plan Calls booked

• Social touchpoints published

title: Part-time Online Math Tutor (Work From Home!)
description:
Brighterly is a dynamic EdTech startup on a mission to transform how children learn and love math. We provide a world-class after-school math excellence program for students from kindergarten to grade 8 in the USA.



We believe math can shape a child’s future — giving them the confidence, skills, and curiosity to excel in school and beyond. Our lessons are not just about solving problems; they’re about opening doors to a bright tomorrow.



As Part Of Our Team, You Will:

- Deliver fun, engaging, and interactive 1-on-1 math lessons using our ready-made scripts and materials.

- Share valuable feedback with our office team to continually improve lessons.

- Provide detailed reports on your students’ progress.

- Keep students motivated and engaged in every session.

- Bring your creativity to the table to enhance learning experiences.



What You’ll Love About Working With Us:

- Flexible schedule — work from home and choose hours that suit you.

- Comprehensive training to prepare you for success.

- All lesson materials provided — focus on teaching, not creating content.

- Supportive environment with ongoing guidance from experienced mentors.

- Fun, youthful, and friendly team culture.

- Opportunities to share and implement your ideas.

- Professional growth — regular training to sharpen your skills.



What We’re Looking For:

- Advanced English (C1+) with a clear, neutral accent.

- Passion for working with young children.

- Minimum 6 months’ experience teaching young learners.

- High school-level math proficiency (or higher) with a diploma.

- Strong communication and problem-solving skills.

- Ability to work weekends and/or weekday afternoons and evenings.

- Self-employed status.

- Patience, punctuality, and a positive attitude.



If you’re passionate about making math exciting and empowering for children, we’d love to have you on board!
