title: Online Business Manager — "The Finisher"
description:
Online Business Manager — "The Finisher"



(The Person Who Makes Sure It Actually Gets Done.)



This is not a VA role.

This is not a "just follow instructions" role.

This is not a project manager who makes Gantt charts nobody reads.



This is the person who catches ideas from a fast-moving founder and turns them into finished, working reality — every single day.



If you've ever worked with a creative, visionary founder and thought

"I LOVE this energy — just hand me the ball and I'll run it to the end zone"

— keep reading.





1. The Short Version (If You're Skimming)



- SaaS company, already live, already paying customers (~40 clients)

- The founder moves fast, starts things, has 100 ideas a day — you LOVE that

- You take what he starts and FINISH it. Every time.

- You run daily operations: check on clients, update the scoreboard, keep things humming

- You don't wait for instructions — you find what needs doing and do it

- You communicate proactively — short updates, questions, progress

- You are trusted, not micromanaged



If someone else's energy and pace drains you, this will be exhausting.

If someone else's energy and pace FUELS you — because it means you always have something meaningful to finish — this is your dream role.





2. Why This Role Exists



I'm the founder. I'm great at starting things. I'm terrible at finishing them.



I have a lead developer (Karlo) who ships code daily — he thrives with my pace.

I have a salesperson (Trey) executing outreach.

What I'm missing: the person who takes my half-built projects, unfinished systems, and started-but-abandoned processes and pushes them across the finish line.



I don't need someone to slow me down. I don't need someone to "hold me accountable."

I need someone who gets EXCITED when I hand them something 60% done and say

"Can you take this from here?"



Because finishing things is what gives you life. It's your genius.





3. What You'll Actually Do



Daily Operations (~40%)

- Check client health dashboard — are videos processing, clips generating, content scheduling?

- Update the weekly scoreboard (KPIs, pipeline, revenue metrics)

- Monitor client communications — flag anything that needs attention

- Keep the CRM/pipeline current

- Be the first to know when something's off — because you're looking every day



Project Completion (~40%)

- Take Charlie's started-but-unfinished projects and drive them to DONE

- Push half-built campaigns, sequences, processes, and systems to completion

- Follow up on things that fell through the cracks

- Track what's in flight, what's stuck, what needs Charlie's input vs. what you can finish yourself

- Make sure nothing stays at 80% — your job is the last 20%



Communication & Coordination (~20%)

- Send daily updates without being asked

- Coordinate between Charlie, Karlo (dev), and Trey (sales)

- Communicate with clients when needed (written, async — not cold calls)

- Ask questions early when something is unclear

- Flag problems WITH solutions, not just problems





4. How You'll Work With Me (The Founder)



I'm highly creative. I move fast. I change direction. I start things.



I am:

- full of ideas (sometimes too many)

- fast-moving and high-energy

- not interested in micromanaging you

- sometimes vague ("Make this happen" without step-by-step)

- focused on what's NEXT, not what's current



What I need from you:

- You LOVE catching my ideas and running with them

- You don't need me to tell you what to do every morning — you already know

- You push things to done while I'm already on to the next thing

- You keep the scoreboard current so I can see reality at a glance

- You tell me "Done" more than you ask "What should I do?"

- When I hand you something half-finished, your reaction is "YES, I'll take it from here"



The Energy Test



Karlo (our lead developer) thrives working with me. He catches my vision fast, ships daily, and never waits for hand-holding. He gets ENERGY from the pace.



I need the operations version of Karlo.



If reading this makes you think "that sounds exhausting" — this isn't your role, and that's okay.

If reading this makes you think "that sounds like every day I've ever wanted" — we should talk.





5. What "Great" Looks Like



After a few months:

- The scoreboard is always current — I never have to ask "where are we?"

- Projects I start get finished — even the ones I forgot I started

- Clients are happy because someone is watching their account daily

- I focus on vision and sales because I trust you with everything else

- Nothing falls through the cracks — because you have a system for catching everything

- We talk about "what's next" and "what shipped" — not "what's stuck"



That's success.





6. The Type of Person Who Thrives Here



This role is for you if:

- You get ENERGY from finishing things — the last 10% is your favorite part

- You love working with creative, fast-moving people (not despite them — BECAUSE of them)

- You create checklists and systems without being asked

- You follow up on everything — nothing gets lost

- You communicate proactively — short updates, no 3-day silences

- You're comfortable talking to clients (written, async)

- You take initiative when something is unclear

- You treat the business like it's yours

- You'd rather finish 5 things than start 10





7. This Role Will Crush You If...



Be honest with yourself.



This is NOT for you if:

- You need step-by-step instructions for everything

- A fast-moving, idea-generating founder feels chaotic to you (not energizing)

- You wait for approval before acting

- You prefer starting projects to finishing them

- You go quiet when you're stuck

- Ambiguity makes you freeze

- You think "that's not my job" when something unexpected lands in your lap

- You need everything planned before anything gets done





8. Tools & Environment



- Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Calendar)

- WhatsApp for daily communication

- Zoom for weekly syncs

- CRM / pipeline tools

- AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) — willingness to learn and use AI is expected

- Our SaaS dashboard (training provided)

- Scoreboard / KPI tracking



No coding required. Our developer handles all technical work.





9. Compensation



- $1,000-1,500 USD / month (based on experience)

- Full-time (40 hrs/week)

- Schedule: Overlap with 8am-5pm CST required

- Time tracking required



This is a growth role. As RWYS scales, this role scales with it.





10. How to Apply



Do not send a resume.



Write a letter (in Microsoft Word, named "finisher - YOUR_NAME") that answers these three questions:



1. Tell me about a time you worked with someone who moves fast and starts a lot of things. How did you handle it? Did you love it or just tolerate it?



2. Describe a specific project or task that someone else started but didn't finish — and you took it over and pushed it to completion. What was it? What made it hard? How did it feel to finish it?



3. What does your ideal daily routine look like in an operations role? Walk me through a typical morning-to-evening workday.



 it to 



Put "finisher" in the subject line.



If you're the person who makes sure it actually gets done —

who finishes what others start, who keeps the system humming,

and who gets ENERGIZED by a fast-moving founder —

this will feel like the role you've been looking for.



If you prefer working at your own pace with detailed instructions, that's okay. This role is not for most people.

title: Bookkeeper/Financial Analyst - Multi-Entity Operations
description:
Location: Remote 

Hours: Full-time, with 2-4 hours overlap with US CST





About Us



We operate a portfolio of technology businesses including an IT consulting firm serving medical/dental practices, a gaming studio, and other ventures. We're transitioning to ERPNext for our accounting operations and need a detail-oriented financial professional to own our bookkeeping and reporting functions across multiple entities.





Core Responsibilities



Daily/Weekly Operations



Reconcile bank statements across multiple business entities

Categorize and track all expenses with detailed spend analysis

Process and record transactions in ERPNext

Maintain clean separation between multiple LLCs and business units

Perform month-end close procedures



Monthly Invoicing & Collections



Generate and send all client invoices on the 1st of every month

Track invoice aging and follow up on overdue accounts

Manage accounts receivable and collection efforts

Coordinate with the operations team on billable services/hours

Maintain customer billing information and terms



Inventory Management



Maintain inventory levels in ERPNext

Monitor inventory costs and usage patterns

Coordinate with the team on reorder points

Track inventory across multiple locations/projects



Financial Reporting



Generate monthly P&L statements for each entity 

Create cost center analysis (e.g., "What is the company costing us monthly?")

Develop profitability reports by business line

Prepare management reports showing cash flow and burn rates

Track project-level profitability for client engagements



Tax Preparation Support



Prepare quarterly federal tax estimate calculations

Prepare state tax estimate calculations (Nebraska primarily)

Organize documentation for CPA review and filing

Maintain tax calendar and ensure deadlines are tracked



Business Intelligence



Identify spending patterns and cost-saving opportunities

Track KPIs across business units

Answer ad-hoc questions like "How much are we spending on software subscriptions?"

Create financial dashboards in ERPNext



Required Qualifications



Must-Haves



3+ years bookkeeping experience with US companies

Experience with multi-entity accounting

Strong understanding of US GAAP

Experience with recurring monthly billing/invoicing

Proficiency in bank reconciliation and financial statement preparation

Experience preparing documentation for US quarterly taxes

Advanced Excel/spreadsheet skills

Excellent written English communication

Available for calls during US business hours as needed



Strongly Preferred



ERPNext experience (or similar ERP systems like Odoo, NetSuite, SAP)

Experience with technology/IT services companies

Inventory management experience

Cost accounting or management accounting background

Experience with project-based profitability tracking

Familiarity with US payroll systems (we use ADP)

Experience with gaming or software development accounting



Technical Requirements



Stable internet connection (must be reliable for 1st of month invoicing)

Comfortable learning new software quickly

Experience with cloud-based accounting tools

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills



What Success Looks Like



All invoices sent by noon CST on the 1st of every month (no exceptions)

Month-end books closed within 3 business days

Quarterly tax packages ready 2 weeks before deadlines

AR aging kept under 30 days average

Inventory counts are accurate and updated weekly

Management has real-time visibility into financial performance

Clean audit trail for all transactions

Proactive identification of financial insights, not just reporting



Growth Opportunities





This role has significant growth potential. As we scale from 16 to 30+ employees, this position could evolve into a Financial Operations Manager role with broader responsibilities and team management opportunities.





To Apply



Upload your resume highlighting US client experience here: 



Message us: 

A brief note explaining your experience with multi-entity accounting

Your experience with monthly billing cycles and inventory management

Your monthly salary expectation in USD

Your time zone and hours of availability

Important: In your application, describe a time when you identified a significant cost-saving opportunity through financial analysis



Applications without US company experience or the cost-saving example will not be considered.





Note: We value honesty and continuous learning. If you don't know something, we expect you to say so and learn it, rather than pretend expertise you don't have. We invest heavily in training the right people.

, and standard office software tools.

title: Data Engineer (Cloud & GIS Focus)
description:
We’re looking for a hands-on Data Engineer who enjoys building things end-to-end — from designing data systems to deploying and running them in production.

If you like owning your work, solving real-world data problems, and building scalable systems in the cloud, this role is for you.

You’ll help us power analytics, AI, and GIS-driven products with reliable, production-grade data infrastructure.



What You’ll Do

Build & Own Data Systems

- Turn business requirements into scalable data solutions

- Design and build clean, maintainable data pipelines (ETL/ELT)

- Develop batch and streaming workflows

- Improve performance, reliability, and monitoring

- Deploy and maintain systems in production



Cloud & Infrastructure

- Work with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Firebase

- Containerize and deploy services using Docker

- Set up CI/CD workflows with GitHub

- Ensure systems are scalable, reliable, and cost-efficient

- Implement logging, monitoring, and data quality checks



Data & Performance

- Design and optimize PostgreSQL schemas and queries

- Implement validation, observability, and reliability checks

- Support AI and GIS-based data workflows



What We’re Looking For

Must-Have

- 2 years of experience in Data Engineering or similar role

- Strong PostgreSQL experience (schema design & performance tuning)

- Solid Python skills

- Experience with GCP

- Docker experience

- GitHub (including CI/CD workflows)

- Ability to independently deploy and maintain production systems



Nice to Have (Big Plus)

Experience with GIS / geospatial tools such as:

- Shapely

- QGIS

- GDAL

- GeoPandas

- H3

- Google Maps API



Experience working with spatial data or map-based analytics is a strong advantage.



Attach a PDF copy of your resume when you submit your application.

title: Data Engineer
description:
We are seeking a highly driven, hands-on Data Engineer to own and execute our end-to-end data architecture — from requirements definition to system design, implementation, deployment, and long-term operations.

You will build and operate scalable, production-grade data infrastructure that powers analytics, AI systems, and GIS workloads. This role requires both strong engineering fundamentals and a DevOps mindset — you will not only build systems, but deploy, monitor, and maintain them in cloud environments.



Key Responsibilities

 End-to-End Data Ownership

- Translate business and product requirements into scalable technical solutions

- Design clean, maintainable data architecture and workflows

- Implement robust ETL/ELT pipelines

- Ensure long-term maintainability, monitoring, and optimization

- Own deployment, reliability, and ongoing operations



Cloud Infrastructure & DevOps

- Architect and manage cloud infrastructure on Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

- Work with Firebase and managed services

- Containerize and deploy services using Docker

- Implement CI/CD workflows using GitHub

- Ensure high availability, scalability, and cost efficiency

- Establish logging, monitoring, and data governance standards



Data Engineering & Systems

- Build and maintain batch and streaming pipelines

- Design and optimize PostgreSQL schemas, indexing, and performance

- Implement data validation, observability, and reliability checks

- Support AI-driven and GIS-based data workflows



Must-Have Technical Skills

Hands-on production experience with:

- PostgreSQL (schema design, indexing, performance tuning)

- Python (modern dependency management, uv preferred)

- Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

- Firebase

- Docker (containerization & deployment)

- GitHub (version control, CI/CD workflows)

You must be capable of independently deploying and maintaining systems in production.



Nice to Have (Strong Plus)

Experience with GIS / Geospatial systems, such as:

- Shapely

- QGIS

- GDAL

- GeoPandas

- H3

- Google Maps API

Experience working with spatial datasets, spatial indexing, or map-based analytics is a significant advantage.



Experience Requirements

- 3–5 years of experience in Data Engineering or related roles

- Strong foundation in data systems and backend software engineering

- Experience building and operating production-grade systems

- Experience working with cloud-native architectures



Soft Skills

- Fluent in English (written and spoken)

- Clear, structured communicator

- Collaborative, low-ego, team-first mindset

- Self-driven and proactive

- Strong ownership mentality — you finish what you start

- Comfortable leveraging AI-assisted coding tools to improve productivity



Preferred Background

- Experience working in remote or distributed teams

- Startup or fast-paced environment experience

- Exposure to AI-coding



Submit your application along with your resume and portfolio link in a google drive link.

title: Shopify Web Developer - CRO Specialist - Ecommerce Advertising Agency - Full Time 3b
description:
APPLY ON MY WEBSITE TO BE CONSIDERED: 





CRO Shopify Web Developer – Job Overview

I am looking for an experienced shopify web developer to help me upgrade client’s checkout processes and execute CRO tests for various ecommerce brands.

In this role you will update Shopify theme templates, develop landing pages using various page builders and html/CSS frameworks, research/install various checkout/bundle applications, and implement A/B tests using testing tools like intelligems.

You will also report on test performance, and provide suggestions for next steps.

This is a full-time opportunity. Training will be provided and you will work directly with me.



About Zack Miller

My name is Zack Miller, and I’ve been a DTC Ecommerce Growth Marketer since 2019. I’ve done everything fro
dia buying and client strategy, to agency internal operations.

I’ve contributed to the growth of over 200 ecommerce brands in my agency experience. Now, as an independent growth marketing partner, I work closely with a small number of brands on their new customer acquisition strategy.

title: Podcast Video Editor
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He knows what makes people watch. He knows pacing, storytelling, and how to build a video that holds attention from start to finish. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our editing team and looking for someone overseas who is hungry, talented, and ready to be pushed.



What this role will do for you

Most editing jobs have you cutting the same content on autopilot every week. This one is different.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is honest and direct. Our process has been shaped by billions of views of content that actually performed.



After one year with us, you will be a significantly better editor. You will understand long-form storytelling and podcast pacing at a level most editors never reach. You will have the work to prove it.



We do not just want to hire you. We want to develop you.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as the relationship grows.



What you will be editing

You own the long-form episode from start to finish. Each week you will deliver fully edited podcast episodes — multicam footage, professional audio, color, and pacing. Finished files are delivered through Google Drive.



You will start with 3 stringouts per week, growing to 6 or more as the relationship develops and capacity expands.



Your Senior Editor reviews every cut and gives direct feedback before anything goes to the client. Revisions are part of the job and are not paid separately. The editors who thrive here study the feedback, apply it fast, and hold themselves to a high standard before they submit anything.



What we need from you

Adobe Premiere Pro

Fast, reliable internet

Available during US business hours for communication and revisions



A note on your computer

Editing multicam podcast footage with professional audio is demanding. A slow machine will slow down your delivery and make this job hard. Before you apply, confirm your setup can handle this work.



Minimum specs:

Processor: Intel i7/i9, AMD Ryzen 7/9, or Apple M1 Pro / M2 / M3 or newer

RAM: 32GB minimum, 64GB preferred

Storage: 1TB SSD minimum — you will regularly receive and store large project files

GPU: Dedicated card with 8GB+ VRAM (PC) or Apple Silicon



In your application video, show us your setup or walk us through your specs.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, tell us how long you have been editing, show us your best long-form work, and tell us why this role interests you. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



The video matters more than your resume.

title: Tax Administration Coordinator (CPA Liaison)
description:
Aligned Advisors | Oceanside, CA (Remote – PST Hours Required)





About Aligned Advisors



Aligned Advisors (
) is a specialized advisory firm serving dental professionals nationwide. We provide integrated services across accounting, fractional CFO support, tax strategy and compliance, and wealth advisory. Our model is built around precision, proactive planning, and delivering a high-touch client experience to growth-oriented dental practices.



Position Overview



We are seeking a Tax Administration Coordinator (CPA Liaison) to join our team full-time. This role is critical to the success of our tax function, acting as the central point of coordination between Aligned Advisors, our clients, and external CPA firms.



This individual will own the tax workflow process end-to-end, ensuring all required documentation is collected, organized, and delivered accurately and on time. You will play a key operational role in driving efficiency, accountability, and communication across all parties involved in tax preparation.



This is a remote role, but candidates must be available during Pacific Time US (PST) business hours.





Key Responsibilities



- Tax Workflow Management



- Own and manage the full lifecycle of tax return coordination



- Track deadlines and ensure timely delivery of all required documents to CPA partners



- Monitor return progress and proactively follow up to avoid delays



- CPA & Internal Liaison



- Serve as the primary point of contact between Aligned Advisors and external CPA firms



- Translate requests between CPAs, internal tea
mbers, and clients



- Ensure CPAs receive complete, accurate, and organized information



- Client Coordination



- Communicate with clients to request, clarify, and follow up on tax documentation



- Ensure a smooth, professional, and timely client experience throughout tax season



- Document Management



- Upload, organize, and maintain files within ShareFile (or similar systems)



- Ensure all documentation is properly labeled, complete, and accessible



- Maintain clean, audit-ready digital records



- Process Optimization



- Identify inefficiencies and help improve tax workflow systems



- Assist in building standardized processes and checklists





Qualifications



Required:



- 2+ years of experience in a tax, accounting, or administrative support role



- Strong understanding of tax preparation workflows and documentation requirements



- Exceptional organizational and project management skills



- High attention to detail and follow-through



- Strong written and verbal communication skills



- Experience working with document management systems (e.g., ShareFile, Google Drive, etc.)



- Ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously



Preferred:



- Experience working with CPA firms or tax preparation teams



- Familiarity with dental or healthcare clients (not required but a plus)



- Experience in a remote, fast-paced professional services environment





What Success Looks Like



- Tax returns are consistently delivered on time with minimal CPA back-and-forth



- Documentation is complete, clean, and easy for CPAs to process



- Clients experience proactive, clear communication



- Internal team spends less time chasing information and resolving bottlenecks





Why Join Aligned Advisors



- Work with a high-performing, growth-focused team



- Exposure to CFO-level strategy and advisory work



- Clear processes, strong leadership, and a culture of accountability



- Opportunity to grow within a specialized niche serving dental entrepreneurs

title: Short Form Video Editor
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He understands what stops the scroll, what keeps people watching, and what makes short-form content actually perform. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our editing team and looking for a short-form editor overseas who is fast, precise, and obsessed with the craft.



What this role will do for you

Short-form editing is not just cutting a clip and slapping on captions. Done right, it is one of the most technical and creative skills in content production.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is direct. You will be pushed to understand why certain 
nts work and others do not, and you will get better faster here than almost anywhere else.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as the relationship grows.



What you will be editing

You do not pick the clips. Your Senior Editor or Account Manager identifies the 
nts from each episode. Your job is to make them hit.



Each clip is 60 seconds or under with clean captions, tight cuts, minimal motion graphics. The standard is high.



You will start with approximately 15 clips per week, growing to 30 or more as volume scales. At the production level required for this role, that is very achievable. We will grow your hours with you.



Finished clips are delivered through 
 for review before anything goes to the client. Revisions are part of the job and are not paid separately. Study the feedback. Apply it. Raise your standard before you submit.



One honest note: understanding what resonates with an American business audience takes time. The way a 
nt needs to be framed, paced, and hooked for this audience is specific. Your early cuts will often come back with feedback. That is expected and built into our process. The editors who grow fastest here are the ones who study American short-form content, pay attention to what performs, and treat every round of feedback as a lesson rather than a correction.



What we need from you

CapCut (primary tool for this role)

Adobe Premiere Pro (required)

Fast, reliable internet

Available during US business hours for communication and revisions



A note on your computer

Short-form editing at this production level is not as hardware-intensive as long-form, but your machine still needs to handle it cleanly. Before you apply, confirm your setup is solid.



Minimum specs:

Processor: Intel i5/i7, AMD Ryzen 5/7, or Apple M1 or newer

RAM: 16GB minimum

Storage: 512GB SSD minimum

Stable internet for file transfers and 



In your application video, show us your setup or walk us through your specs.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, tell us how long you have been editing, show us short-form work you are proud of, and tell us why this role interests you. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



The video matters more than your resume.

title: State Integrations & Compliance Manager | Fully Remote | U.S. State Agency Integrations
description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a State Integrations & Compliance Manager to lead the acquisition and management of API integrations with U.S. state agencies (DMV/BMV/DPS).

This is a high-impact role focused on unlocking access to government data systems and building scalable integrations across all states.

You will own the full integration lifecycle from identifying APIs and securing access to ensuring compliance and ongoing system reliability.



**What You’ll Do

*API Integrations & Access (Core Focus)

- Identify APIs, data feeds, and integration methods across states

- Determine access types (REST, SOAP, SFTP, portals, third-party providers)

- Contact agencies to validate access and onboarding requirements

- Build and maintain a structured database of integrations



*Integration Ownership

- Own the full lifecycle: discovery ? outreach ? application ? approval

- Secure API credentials, sandbox access, and production approvals

- Coordinate technical onboarding (IP whitelisting, credentials, etc.)



*Agency Communication

- Act as the main point of contact for state agencies

- Communicate on both operational and basic technical topics

- Drive consistent follow-ups to move integrations forward



*Compliance & Documentation

- Manage applications and compliance documentation (DPPA, security, etc.)

- Ensure all records are accurate and audit-ready

- Support audits and regulatory requirements



*What We’re Looking For

- Experience with APIs, integrations, or technical onboarding

- Strong organization and ability to manage multiple workflows

- Comfortable working with government systems or compliance processes

- Strong communication skills (technical + non-technical)

- Highly persistent and detail-oriented



*Success in This Role Looks Like

- API integrations secured and activated across multiple states

- Consistent onboarding progress with no delays

- Strong relationships with agency contacts

- Fully compliant, audit-ready documentation



*Why Join Us?

- Fully remote

- Work directly with U.S. government systems

- High-impact role in a fast-growing company

- Opportunity to build expertise in integrations + compliance

title: Financial Analyst
description:
TO APPLY PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK: 



*About Spotter Labs, Inc.

Spotter Labs, Inc. is a technology-enabled transportation platform focused on improving operational performance, financial efficiency, and profitability across a rapidly growing trucking ecosystem. We combine advanced analytics, AI-driven tools, and disciplined execution to support carriers, owner-operators, and enterprise logistics customers.

As the company scales, we are expanding our finance team to support data-driven decision making, improved financial visibility, and strong operating discipline.



*Position Overview

The Financial Analyst will work directly with the CFO to support financial planning, analysis, reporting, governance, and operational decision support across the organization. This is a high-visibility role requiring strong analytical capability, financial modeling expertise, and disciplined execution.

The role combines financial analysis with structured oversight responsibilities to help ensure the company maintains accurate reporting, documentation, and governance standards as it scales.



**Key Responsibilities

*Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)

- Build and maintain financial models supporting budgeting, forecasting, and scenario planning

- Analyze revenue, margin, and cost drivers across business units

- Track key performance indicators and operating metrics

- Support annual planning and rolling forecast processes



*Performance Reporting

- Prepare weekly, monthly, and quarterly financial reporting packages

- Perform variance analysis against budget, forecast, and prior periods

- Develop dashboards and reporting frameworks to improve visibility and decision-making



*Operational & Strategic Analysis

- Partner with operations leaders to assess profitability and cost efficiency

- Support pricing analysis, unit economics, and customer profitability initiatives

- Conduct ad hoc analysis related to fleet performance, labor, and growth initiatives



*Governance & Documentation Oversight

- Maintain and update internal governance documents, financial policies, and reporting frameworks

- Assist in preparing and maintaining board materials, resolutions, and executive documentation

- Ensure accuracy and version control of financial models and reporting templates

- Coordinate updates to governance documentation required by lenders, investors, auditors, or regulatory bodies

- Support the CFO in maintaining compliance documentation across corporate entities



*Executive Support

- Assist the CFO with executive, board, and investor reporting materials

- Support due diligence for acquisitions and financing initiatives

- Contribute to the development of scalable financial processes and internal controls



*Required Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field

- 3–6 years of experience in FP&A, corporate finance, investment banking, or related analytical roles

- Strong financial modeling and advanced Excel skills

- Experience handling confidential financial and governance materials

- Ability to turn complex data into clear, actionable insights

- Strong attention to detail and structured execution discipline



*Preferred Qualifications

- Experience in logistics, transportation, SaaS, or technology-enabled businesses

- Familiarity with KPI-driven operating environments

- Experience supporting board reporting or investor communications

- Exposure to M&A or operational finance analysis



*Key Attributes for Success

- Analytical and detail-oriented

- Comfortable working directly with senior leadership

- Strong organizational discipline and documentation rigor

- High level of discretion and professionalism

- Self-starter capable of operating with limited structure



*Why Join Spotter Labs

- Direct exposure to executive leadership and strategic decision-making

- Opportunity to help build scalable finance and governance infrastructure in a high-growth environment

- Broad visibility into operations, technology, and growth initiatives

- Performance-oriented culture where accountability and results matter



Candidates who score 75% or higher on the assessment will automatically advance to the next stage of the selection process and be scheduled for an interview with the CFO. Following that interview, a final hiring decision will be made within one week.

title: Accountant/Bookkeeper - US GAAP
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Accountant with strong inventory accounting experience to oversee our financial operations, manage inventory accounting processes, and ensure compliance with US GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles). This is a full-time, remote position, offering an excellent opportunity to contribute to the financial oversight of a growing tech company while enjoying the flexibility of working from home.



This role requires demonstrated expertise in inventory accounting, including valuation, reconciliation, reporting, and internal controls. Candidates without hands-on inventory accounting experience will not be considered.



**Key Responsibilities:

*Inventory Accounting (Primary Focus)

- Manage and maintain accurate inventory records, including tracking, costing, adjustments, and reconciliations.

- Oversee inventory valuation in compliance with US GAAP (FIFO, weighted average, etc., as applicable).

- Perform regular inventory reconciliations between accounting records and operational systems.

- Monitor inventory reserves, obsolescence, and shrinkage.

- Partner with operations to ensure accurate inventory reporting and controls.

- Support audit processes related to inventory balances and procedures.



*Day-to-Day Accounting

- Manage daily accounting operations including accounts payable/receivable, general ledger entries, bank reconciliations, and expense tracking.

- Ensure proper recording of inventory-related transactions within the general ledger.



*Financial Reporting

- Prepare accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports, ensuring compliance with GAAP standards.

- Provide detailed inventory reporting and analysis as part of financial close.



*Record Maintenance

- Maintain organized and up-to-date financial records, receipts, and documentation for all transactions.



*Budget Support

- Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis, particularly inventory-related cost forecasting.



*Compliance Assurance

- Ensure financial practices comply with applicable local, state, and federal regulations.



*Process Improvement

- Recommend improvements to inventory and accounting workflows, tools, and systems to increase efficiency and accuracy.



*Tax Support

- Assist with preparation and organization of financial data for tax filings, particularly inventory-related schedules, and work with external tax professionals as needed.



*Software Management

- Use financial software such as QuickBooks Online to manage company accounts and generate reports.



*Internal Controls

- Implement and maintain strong internal controls over inventory and financial reporting to safeguard company assets and prevent discrepancies.



**Qualifications:

*Required:

- Strong, hands-on experience in inventory accounting, including valuation, reconciliation, and reporting.

- Solid understanding of US GAAP as it relates to inventory and cost accounting.

- Experience managing inventory within an accounting system.

- Proficiency in accounting software, particularly QuickBooks Online.

- Advanced Excel skills.

- Exceptional attention to detail with a proven track record of accuracy in reconciliations and financial reporting.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

- Highly organized with excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Capable of working independently and dependably in a remote setting.

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field required.



*Preferred:

- Certification (e.g., CPA, CMA, CB).

- Prior experience in a startup or technology-driven environment.

- Experience supporting financial audits involving inventory.



*Compensation & Benefits:

- The ability to work remotely from anywhere.

- Opportunity to work with a dynamic, fast-growing team in the AI and analytics space.



*Hiring Process:

Candidates who score 75% or higher on the assessment will automatically advance to the next stage of the selection process and be scheduled for an interview with the CFO. Following that interview, a final hiring decision will be made within one week.

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer
description:
You know MrBeast. Now work with the person who helped direct it.



Our founder spent years as a video director at MrBeast, working on content that generated over 10 billion views. He knows what makes someone click. He knows what stops the scroll, what creates curiosity, and what a thumbnail needs to do before a single second of video plays. That knowledge drives everything we do at Biggest Agency.



We produce high-end YouTube shows and podcasts for entrepreneurs and business leaders. We are growing our creative team and looking for a thumbnail designer overseas who understands that a great thumbnail is not decoration. It is the first sale.



What this role will do for you

Most design jobs have you executing someone else’s vision with no context for whether it worked. This one is different.



You will see real performance data. You will know your CTR. You will run A/B tests in YouTube Studio and track what actually makes people click. Over time you will develop an instinct for high-performing thumbnail design that most designers never get the chance to build.



Our standards are high. Our feedback is direct. You will be pushed to think like a strategist, not just a designer.



This role starts part-time with a clear path to full-time as volume scales.



What you will be designing

You design three thumbnails per episode. For each one you will receive a brief, a transcript, and the episode trailer to work from. You pitch your concepts to the team before you build. Once approved, you deliver the finals.



You will start at 9 thumbnails per week (approximately 10 hours), scaling to 36 or more as the agency grows and your capacity expands.



Beyond design, you are part of the performance loop. You will set up A/B tests in YouTube Studio, monitor CTR across shows, and report findings back to the team. If something is not clicking, we need to know why and what to try next. That is part of your job.



You will collaborate closely with our editors and account managers. Thumbnails do not live in isolation. They connect to the episode title, the trailer, and the overall show brand. We expect you to understand that relationship and design accordingly.



What we need from you

Canva (required)

Adobe Photoshop and/or Illustrator (strongly preferred)

Proven experience designing YouTube thumbnails specifically — not just general graphic design

Understanding of CTR, YouTube thumbnail best practices, and what drives clicks

Ability to concept, pitch, and execute — not just take direction

Comfortable working inside YouTube Studio for A/B testing and analytics

Available during US business hours for feedback and collaboration

Fast, reliable internet



What great looks like in this role

You study thumbnails obsessively. You know which channels are winning on YouTube right now and why. You can look at a transcript and immediately see the angle that will make someone click. You take feedback without ego and apply it faster than expected. You track your own performance and bring data to conversations, not just opinions.



You treat every thumbnail like it is the most important one you have ever made. Because for that client, it is.



To apply

Record a 2 to 3 minute video. Introduce yourself, show us your best YouTube thumbnail work, and walk us through one thumbnail you are proud of... what was the brief, what was your thinking, and what did it perform like if you have the data. Upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and include the link.



Also include a link to your portfolio. The work matters more than your resume.

title: Talent Manager | Own Our Hiring Engine for Fastest Growing Company in Canada
description:
About AMZ Prep:



AMZ Prep is a global logistics and fulfillment platform powering fast growing omnichannel brands.



We operate a distributed warehouse network across North America and Europe, supporting Amazon FBA, DTC, retail, B2B, and middle mile freight. Our clients depend on speed, precision, and operational intelligence to scale.



We are not building a traditional 3PL.



We are building a technology enabled logistics platform where systems, data, and automation create leverage. AI is core to that future.



Our Core Values



These define how we operate and who succeeds here.



Win Relentlessly

Hard working and resilient, even when things get tough. Willing to go the extra mile to deliver results. Obsessed with winning and raising the bar every day. Growth minded and never satisfied with good enough. We move fast, adapt fast, and outwork everyone to get it done. We win as a team, and we help our merchants win online too. We are obsessed with both.



Own the Outcome

Dependable. You get your work done. No excuses, no oversight needed. Others can rely on you to deliver every time. You own results, good or bad, and step up when it counts most. You build trust through consistency, accountability, and follow through.



Be Humbly Confident

Confident but grounded. Direct but respectful. No drama. No politics. No BS. We call out what does not add up openly and politely. We take feedback, admit mistakes, and keep improving. We win together, not just as individuals.



Obsess Over Data

Data drives every decision, conversation, and strategy for our business and our merchants. We use data to identify problems faster, make smarter decisions, and help our brands win online. We test, measure, and refine constantly. We turn analytics into insights, not just numbers.



If these do not resonate, this role is not for you.



Recruiting Manager Scorecard weekly:

About This Role



As a Talent Partner at AMZ Prep, you own the full recruiting life cycle - from sourcing and pipeline management through to second-round interviews. You'll work closely with hiring managers, leverage the founder's LinkedIn presence for inbound recruiting, and keep every candidate warm until a decision is made. You'll also own and administer our internal CultureTest platform, the proprietary assessment tool used to evaluate candidate fit before final rounds.



This role sits at the intersection of recruiting, candidate experience, and people operations. Major metrics you'll manage and oversee:



Interviews (calls) per week

Qualified candidates advanced to stage 2

# of active qualified candidates per role

% of candidates touched weekly (no one goes dark

Time to move candidate to stage 2

What You'll Do



Sourcing & Pipeline



Build and execute multi-channel sourcing strategies: LinkedIn Recruiter, job boards, direct outreach, CRM campaigns, and social media

Leverage the founder's LinkedIn profile as an active recruiting channel - engage inbound interest, post role updates, and drive awareness with target talent pools

Collaborate with the sales team to identify referrals and warm connections in target talent networks

Build and maintain skilled, diverse candidate pools for every active role

Target: 20-40 candidate calls per week, with a goal of identifying 3-5 qualified candidates per role per week that fit our values, budget and goals.

Interviewing



Own first-round and second-round interviews across all open roles

Conduct structured screens to assess skills, experience, compensation expectations, availability, and culture fit

Provide written candidate scorecards and verbal feedback to hiring managers after every round

Protect hiring manager time - they should only see candidates who have cleared two rounds

Candidate Experience & Warm Pipeline



Keep every candidate in the pipeline warm - proactive outreach, status updates, and relationship-building at every stage

No candidate goes cold or goes silent on your watch

Build relationships with passive candidates for future roles even when there's no immediate opening

Provide weekly updates to candidates focused on transparency and candor

ATS & Platform Management



Own the ATS end-to-end: job postings, candidate records, pipeline stages, and reporting

Administer the AMZ Prep CultureTest platform: configure assessments per role, track completion rates, score results, and surface insights to hiring managers

Maintain clean, accurate data at all times

External Recruiter Coordination



Brief and manage agency and contract recruiters on active roles

Set quality bar expectations, track pipeline output, and hold external partners accountable

Reporting & Strategy



Weekly pipeline report to leadership: calls completed, candidates advanced, pipeline health per role, blockers

Participate in hiring strategy sessions and follow-up meetings with hiring managers

What You'll Need



Bachelor's degree and 2-5 years of full-cycle recruiting experience

Comfortable owning first and second-round interviews across multiple roles simultaneously

Strong sourcing skills: LinkedIn Recruiter, ATS/CRM tools, job boards, and social media

Experience managing and growing a founder or executive's LinkedIn as a recruiting channel is a strong plus

ATS experience - sourcing, tracking, and managing candidates with clean data hygiene

High-volume comfortable: 20-40 calls per week is normal, not heavy

Exceptional communication - candidates should feel attended to at every stage

Self-directed, organized, and strong prioritization skills

Discretion with confidential candidate and compensation information

Bonus: logistics, ecommerce, or high-growth operations background

Job Types: Full-time, Fixed term contract, Internship / Co-op



Application question(s):





All candidates you recruit at AMZ Prep are required to complete our CultureTest as part of the hiring process. As the owner of recruiting, you would be responsible for managing and administering this step.

Please complete the CultureTest below as part of your application:











Once completed, briefly confirm and give feedback of what you'd improve. Please ensure you record a 1-minute Loom video introducing yourself and why you’re a strong fit for this role.

title: Virtual Assistant | Coding Experience Needed | Dance Academy - Salsa/Bachata
description:
Full-time, long-term opportunity.



No virtual assistant experience needed.



Looking for tech-savvy people with coding experience.



Environment here is CREATIVE and FUN.



ABOUT US

We are the largest salsa & bachata dance academy in Los Angeles and are always expanding.



Busy times!



The culture here is FUN and friendly. Our goal is to make every student feel welcome and completely taken care of all the time.



Dancing changes lives - we are on a mission to deliver the best salsa/bachata educational experience on Earth.



The company is MaS Dance Academy.



YOUR FOCUS:

"Virtual Assistant"



This role has much a wider variety of tasks than working in a large corporation, which makes it more challenging but also more fun.



Some examples:

• Automating procedures (with or without A.I.)

• Website updates

• CRM development

• App updates (built on React Native - experience not required)

• Creating beautiful, concise, clean reports,

• Research

• Etc.



Our culture is fast-paced and every day is unique.



Although you'll begin by doing what is asked, over time (and the sooner the better), you'll need to pro-actively and independently handle tasks/projects without being told and recommend new projects based on opportunities you see. For a smart, motivated person, this role will be more stimulating than a traditional virtual assistant role.





SCHEDULE

9:00am - 6:00 PM PST (Los Angeles time) Monday - Friday





GROWTH:

Payment increases over time.



If you are outstanding, long-term there may also be the opportunity to be promoted into a leadership role





TRAINING:

You'll receive basic training but many things you'll need to figure out on the fly. You must be a quick learner with a "can do" attitude.





COMPANY CULTURE

Working in a small business like ours is very different from a large corporation. Some of the differences are:



• More creativity: you'll handle a wide variety of tasks and projects. This is more challenging than just following strict templates, but also more stimulating and exciting.



• Impact: your actions will have a big impact on the business, which you will clearly see



• Feedback: your feedback on what we are doing well, and what could be better, is not only valued, but is mandatory! We want everyone here thinking as if they were the business owner.





ABOUT YOU:

• Excellent written English (show this in your application!)

• Coding experience

• Technical - great with computers

• Good listener - only needs to be told once

• Strong communicator (you must OVERcommunicate with management and tea
mbers)

• Smart, quick learner

• Fastidious - attention to detail

• Decent at basic math

• Pro-active - anticipates what may be helpful and goes above and beyond to contribute

• Meticulous, organized, methodical.

• Reliable! Always shows up and focuses.

• Critical, creative thinker. Not robotic. Always looking for better ways to do things. Always considering all the angles of a task.

• Willing to share your opinion - we NEED to hear your opinions

• Able to ask clarifying questions in order to get tasks right the first time

• Always eager to improve yourself and the company’s systems. Habitually thinks strategically and takes action for improvements.

• Self-starter

• Independent decision maker

• Flexible - can quickly adjust on the fly

• Comfortable with excel

• Fast computer

• Fast, dependable Internet

• Bonus if you have experience or interest in salsa or bachata!



APPLY NOW. FORMAT YOUR APPLICATION AS A NUMBERED LIST WITH THE FOLLOWING:



1. A brief intro about yourself



2. Can you work the hours requested?



3. Complete this form: 
/
. Write in the subject line of your application: 1. Grammar score 2. Typing WPM 3. Numbers assessment answer (regarding achieving $2,000 profit)



5. What coding education or experience do you have? (Javascript, HTML, CSS, React Native, Zoho CRM , Zoho Deluge, or anything else?)



6. These positions are competitive, often with hundreds of applicants. What is your competitive requested monthly payment for this full-time position?





(Don't
 me off the platform or message on social media - your application will be disqualified. Don't worry, I personally check every application here)



Looking forward to meeting you!



Danny







---------

description:
Virtual Assistant – Order Processing & Customer Support 



We are looking for a reliable **Virtual Assistant** to help with order processing and customer support for a busy U.S.-based service company. This role requires someone who is organized, responsive, and comfortable communicating with restaurants and customers.

**Requirements**

description:

title: Inbound SDR / Appointment Setter (High-Performance Sales Role – Remote)
description:
**** JOB OVERVIEW



We are a fast-growing U.S.-based credit repair company looking for a highly motivated Inbound Sales Development Representative (SDR) to handle organic inbound leads and convert them into qualified live transfers.



This is NOT a general VA role.



This is a performance-based sales position for someone who understands urgency, metrics, and execution.



You will be responsible for:



Calling inbound leads immediately

Qualifying prospects

Live transferring them to a closer



We use GoHighLevel CRM and structured follow-up systems.



****Who This Role Is For



This role is for someone who:



Is competitive and wants to win daily

Understands sales metrics and performance

Takes ownership of results — not just tasks

Is disciplined with follow-up and CRM usage

Wants to grow into a closer role over time



If you are looking for a basic VA or support role, this is NOT for you.



****Responsibilities

Call new inbound leads within 5 minutes of submission

Execute structured 5-day follow-up cadence (calls + SMS)

Qualify prospects using script and criteria

Live transfer qualified leads to closer

Log all activity accurately inside GoHighLevel

Maintain clean pipeline and dispositions

Track and report daily performance metrics



****Performance Expectations



We are a metrics-driven environment. You must be comfortable operating at a high level.



You will be expected to maintain:



Fast speed-to-lead (critical)

20–35%+ connect rate (as volume scales)

Strong qualification rate

Consistent live transfers daily



You should already understand:



Dials per day

Connect rate

Transfer / set rate

CRM discipline



****We track performance daily. If you are not hitting activity and effort expectations, this role will not be a fit.



**Requirements

Experience as an SDR / Appointment Setter / Live Transfer Rep

Experience using dialers

CRM experience (GoHighLevel or HubSpot preferred)

Strong spoken English

Quiet work environment

Reliable high-speed internet

Sales mindset (not just reading scripts)



****Compensation

Base Pay: $7–$9/hour (based on experience)

Per Qualified Live Transfer Bonus: $3–$10+ per transfer

Monthly Performance Bonuses



Top SDRs on our team consistently:



Generate high daily transfer volume

Earn significantly above base pay

Increase earnings based on performance



****This role is performance-driven — the more you produce, the more you earn.



****Growth Opportunity



Top performers in this role have the opportunity to:



Move into a closer position

Increase compensation significantly

Take on leadership or training roles



We promote based on performance — not time.



**IMPORTANT



We are only bringing on 1–2 SDRs for this role.



We are looking for someone who can perform at a high level immediately.



**** To Apply (Required)



Please answer ALL of the following:



How many calls per day did you average in your last role?

What was your average connect rate?

What was your average transfer or appointment set rate?

What CRM and dialer have you used?

What was your highest performing day or week (metrics)?



**** Send a 60-second voice recording introducing yourself.



**** Put “007” in the subject line so we know you read the full description.



** Applications without complete answers will NOT be reviewed.

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
We are seeking a highly capable, proactive Executive Assistant to directly support the CEO. This is a high-impact role for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, operates with an owner’s mindset, and is passionate about contributing to meaningful work—particularly within education, schools, and mission-driven organizations. This individual will serve as a strategic partner to the CEO, helping to streamline operations, manage communications, and create leverage across the business.



Location Requirement:

This role ideally supports a Pacific Time (PT) schedule, or candidates must be willing and able to work within Pacific Time business hours.





Key Responsibilities:



1. CEO Calendar & Schedule Management

* Anticipate scheduling conflicts and proactively resolve them

* Ensure the CEO is prepared for all meetings with relevant materials and context



2.
 & Communication Management

* Draft, respond to, and prioritize
s on behalf of the CEO

3. Research & Strategic Support

* Conduct research on clients, prospects, media opportunities, and industry trends

* Assist in preparing reports, briefs, and internal documents



4. Administrative & Operational Support

* Assist with project coordination and follow-ups across initiatives using Asana

* Track time, tasks, and resource allocation using Harvest



5. Talent Sourcing & Recruiting Support

* Support hiring efforts through LinkedIn recruiting and talent research

* Identify, source, and help screen potential candidates

* Maintain organized candidate pipelines and communication





Required Skills & Experience: 

*  3–5+ years experience as an Executive Assistant or Administrative Assistant supporting senior leadership

* Strong experience with calendar and
 management at an executive level

* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and SharePoint

* Experience with LinkedIn recruiting / talent sourcing

* Experience using project management and time tracking tools such as Asana and Harvest

* Familiarity with AI tools such as ChatGPT and Claude

* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills

* Highly organized with strong attention to detail

* Ability to prioritize, multitask, and operate independently





Preferred Experience:

* Background working with education, schools, or non-profit organizations

* Experience in PR, communications, or agency environments

* Exposure to mission-driven or purpose-led businesses



Core Traits & Mindset:

* Owner’s Mentality – takes initiative, solves problems, and thinks ahead

* Proactive & Resourceful – anticipates needs before they arise

* Aligned with Mission & Values – genuinely cares about the impact of the work

* Heart for Education & Service – passion for contributing to meaningful causes

* Faith-Aligned (Preferred) – values-driven approach to work and relationships

title: Social Media Growth Manager
description:
IMPORTANT: You must reply to each of our 4 questions to be considered.



Also, your application MUST include a direct link to ONE YouTube channel you personally managed. Include the subscriber count when you started and what you grew it to.



Applications without this specific link and these specific numbers will be deleted.



We’ve received many AI-generated replies that copy our job description back to us. Those get deleted immediately. If that's the kind of effort you put into a reply to a long-term position, then that's the effort we assume you would put into the job.



We’re hiring a social media manager, not an agency and not a general VA.



How to apply:



In your response, include:

1. A link to at least one YouTube channel you personally grew, with the subscriber count when you started vs. now

2. Links to any TikTok or Instagram accounts you grew, with follower counts before and after your involvement

3. How you personally grow these channels, your actual strategy, not a list of buzzwords

4. Confirmation that you handle video editing and posting yourself



This job is NOT for you if:

- Your only experience is on smaller YT accounts with less than 25K subscribers, IG accounts with less than 25K followers, or TT accounts with less than 100K followers

- You’re not a geek about algorithms and performance metrics

- You rely on scheduling tools as a growth strategy

- You cannot provide links to specific accounts you’ve grown



This job IS for you if you can:

- Take our raw video content and edit it for YouTube, long-form and Shorts, Instagram, and TikTok

- Edit, post, and optimize for channel growth

- Stay current on platform algorithms and growth strategies

- Be proactive with studying and testing hooks, pacing, captions, and formats

- Maintain strict brand consistency in messaging, tone, colors, and wording

- Meet weekly with the content host



Core responsibilities:



Research: Study top-performing creators and content in our niche. Track what is working and why.



Script Support: Help determine the best marketing hooks and topics based on your research in our industry.



Edit and Upload: Edit videos and publish content across all platforms to maximize reach, retention, and growth.



Requirements:

- 1+ years hands-on experience growing video-based social accounts

- Proven understanding of YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok algorithms

- Strong video editing skills focused on hooks and retention

- Fluent English

- Available during EST hours



This is a secure, long-term role for the right person.

title: Video Creator for Video Ads
description:
DO NOT TELL US TO CHECK YOUR PROFILE - to apply you need to answer our questions below...



We're hiring a Video Creator to build short-form, direct-response video ads for Instagram,
, and TikTok.



This is not an on-camera role. You will not be the face of the brand. Your role is to research competition, create marketing hooks that you believe will drive sales, then turn approved hooks into video ads for us to test.



Product: A dog supplement focused on cognitive health, dementia, and longevity.



Your job would be to:

- Research competitors and current winning ads in the dog health category

- Develop and submit marketing hooks based on your research

- Use approved hooks to create direct-response video ads

- Source or create footage using UGC, stock, B-roll, product clips, voiceover, or AI tools

- Produce multiple variations per concept

- Deliver a minimum of 2 unique video ads per day

- Optimized for 9:16, 1:1 and 4:5

- Participate in weekly meetings with company owners to review hooks, ad ideas, and performance



Video formats and length:

- Short-form ads only

- Typical length: 10 to 60 seconds

- Focus on strong pacing, clear storytelling, and fast execution



This is a high-volume role, not long-form or cinematic production



Requirements:

- Excellent spoken and written English

- Strong direct-response marketing background

- Strong short-form video editing skills

- Clear understanding of pacing, hooks, and attention control

- Ability to produce consistent daily videos

- Experience using AI tools for video or voice is acceptable and encouraged, but not required



IMPORTANT: Please answer all of the following. Applications that do not meet these requirements will not be reviewed:



1. What AI video or voice tools are you currently using in your workflow, and how do you use them? Be specific.

2. Submit examples of video ads you personally created. These must be ads, not general or organic content.

3. Share a link to a short-form video ad, under 60 seconds, that you believe is a strong direct-response ad. Explain why you think it converts viewers to buyers.



This can be a fun, long-term job for someone who understands how successful ads are built, tested, and scaled.

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Job Description

The M&A Sales Development Representative is a strategic growth role at Intelligent Technical Solutions (ITS), focused on identifying and engaging potential acquisition targets. Unlike traditional sales, this role centers on building high-level relationships with IT business owners and industry brokers to discuss the future of their organizations. Your primary goal is to act as a professional ambassador for ITS, initiating conversations that lead to successful mergers or acquisitions. The ideal candidate is a disciplined, remote-first professional who values meaningful engagement over scripted outreach and can communicate with executive-level presence. 



Job Responsibilities

Strategic Conversation Management

Initiate and maintain high-volume outreach to achieve a minimum of 40 quality conversations per day with business owners and decision-makers. 

Broker & Intermediary Networking

Proactively identify and engage with business brokers to build a long-term deal flow of "off-market" acquisition opportunities. 

Growth-Focused Outreach

Communicate the ITS value proposition as a strategic partner, focusing on how companies can join or merge with us to enhance their cybersecurity and operational scale.

Target Qualification

Vet potential acquisition targets based on core criteria, including headcount, existing IT infrastructure, and their openness to strategic transition. 

Smart Outreach for High Conversion

Execute personalized "Smart Outreach" strategies (deep research and tailored follow-ups) specifically for 2nd First Time Appointments (FTAs) to ensure high-quality deal flow. 

Resilient Objection Handling

Navigate common industry pushback by shifting the focus from "selling a service" to "exploring a strategic partnership or exit strategy." 

Pipeline Stewardship

Manage the hand-off of qualified leads to the M&A Director, ensuring the relationship remains warm until the discovery meeting actually takes place. 

CRM Accuracy

Maintain meticulous records in HubSpot, capturing business intelligence and conversation history to support the long-term M&A pipeline. 



Job Qualifications

Professional Presence: Comfortable engaging directly with business owners, CEOs, and seasoned business brokers. 

Outbound Experience: 2+ years in a high-volume outbound role. Business development, recruitment, or similar where building relationships from scratch was part of the job.

Resilience & Discipline: Consistent and self-directed. Able to hit targets in a remote environment, manage a pipeline with minimal supervision, and treat setbacks as part of the process.

Strong Communication: Exceptional verbal and written English, with the ability to explain complex concepts (like M&A or Cybersecurity) in a clear, relatable way.

Technical Adaptability: A fast learner who can quickly become comfortable with MSP terminology and basic financial concepts (e.g., EBITDA, recurring revenue).

Tool Proficiency: Hands-on experience using HubSpot for CRM and ZoomInfo for lead intelligence, or similar tools, is a must.

Collaborative Spirit: A team player who aligns with ITS Core Values. 



Preferred Qualifications

Experience in the Managed Service Provider (MSP) or Cybersecurity industry. 

Previous experience in "Buy-Side" M&A lead generation or business brokerage. 

Background in professional services recruitment. 



Job Requirements

A home office set up that meets the following:

Computer: A working computer with a minimum of 8GB RAM and one of the following:

Windows 11 with an Intel i5 or i7 (8th generation or newer), or

AMD Ryzen 5 or 7 (2000 series or newer), or

macOS 12 or newer.

Internet: Stable internet connection with at least 5 Mbps download and upload speeds.

Audio Equipment: A quality headset with a noise-cancellation feature for meetings.

Mobile Device: A smartphone capable of installing the Microsoft Authenticator app.

Webcam: An HD webcam for video calls.

A very strong cultural fit the ITS way (Our core values will be discussed during the interview) 

Excellent command of the English language both spoken and written 

Able to pass our online behavior and cognitive assessments.

Able to provide NBI Clearance (for PH applicants).



Compensation

Pay rate starts at Php 57,000/month and may vary by experience and location.



Benefits

Comprehensive Health Plans / HMO (Employee & Dependent/s)

Life Insurance

Flexible Benefit Reimbursement (Dental/Vision/Mental Health/Wellness/Government Contributions)

Paid Time Off (starts at 15 days per year)

Maternity/Paternity Leave

Paid US Holiday

Night Shift Bonus

Salary Advancement/Loan

Health & Wellness Program

Company-paid training and certification



APPLY HERE NOW!

title: Full-Time Outbound Caller + Closer for US SaaS Company (Commission-Heavy, High Earners Only)
description:
We're Vault, a US SaaS company. We help contractors recover lost revenue from missed calls. We need one experienced, full-time outbound caller + closer. You must have outbound calling experience . You already know how to dial, pitch, handle objections, and close.



WHAT'S ALREADY BUILT FOR YOU:

- Power dialer loaded and ready (you press call, it dials)

- CRM with full pipeline management

- Lead lists loaded weekly (you never source leads)

- Automated audit system that runs 7 days and generates reports

- US phone number provided

- Word-for-word scripts (you can adjust based on your style)



YOUR 3 JOBS:



1. OUTBOUND — 200+ dials per day minimum:

Call contractors from the power dialer. Offer a free 7-day trial that monitors their missed calls and shows them how much revenue they're losing. You walk them through a 30-second setup on their phone. You're offering something free. Easy yes.



2. FOLLOW UP — report delivery calls:

After 7 days, a report auto-generates showing how many calls the contractor missed and how much money they lost. You call them. Walk them through the numbers. The data is brutal — contractors see $20,000-$100,000/year walking out the door. The report does the convincing.



3. CLOSE — ask for the sale:

After delivering the report you ask: "Want to keep this running? $147 a month." Most say yes because the math is obvious — $147/month to save $50,000+/year. You handle any remaining objections and get the card.



YOU SHOULD APPLY IF:

- You've worked outbound in a BPO, call center, or remote sales role

- You've made 200+ dials/day before and it doesn't scare you

- You know how to handle objections without freezing

- You've closed deals over the phone before (even small ones)

- You don't need someone holding your hand

- You hear "commission-heavy" and get excited, not nervous



DO NOT APPLY IF:

- You've never done outbound calling

- You need extensive training on how to make calls

- You're looking for a high base salary with no performance pressure

- 200 dials/day sounds like a lot to you



PAY STRUCTURE (commission-heavy — designed for closers):



Base: $1.50/hour ($12/day, $250/month floor)



Commissions:

- $10 per free trial you set up and get running

- $75 per trial that converts to a paying customer ($147/month)

- $150 per customer that upgrades to $499/month (happens after a few months, easy upsell)



NO CAP. EVER.



DAILY EARNINGS AT 200+ DIALS:



Solid performer — 6 trials/day, 2 closes/day:

$12 base + $60 trials + $150 closes = $222/day = $4,440/month



Strong performer — 8 trials/day, 3 closes/day:

$12 + $80 + $225 = $317/day = $6,340/month



Animal — 10 trials/day, 4 closes/day:

$12 + $100 + $300 = $412/day = $8,240/month



Weekly pay via Wise or PayPal. Never late.



THE PRODUCT SELLS ITSELF:

The first call is offering something free. The second call is delivering data that shocks them — "you missed 28 calls worth $94,000 last year." You're not convincing anyone of anything. You're showing them their own numbers and asking if they want to keep making money. This is the easiest close you'll ever make.



SCHEDULE:

8 hours/day, Monday-Friday

US business hours (8am-5pm Central, 1-hour break)

200+ dials per day is the minimum expectation. Non-negotiable.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Minimum 6 months outbound calling experience (BPO, call center, remote sales, freelance — any counts)

- Clear English with neutral or American-passing accent

- Quiet workspace, fast internet, quality headset

- Comfortable with power dialers and CRMs

- 200+ dials/day is your normal, not your stretch goal

- Reliable. On time. Every single day.

- Hungry. You see uncapped commissions and you want to destroy your targets.



THIS IS A GROUND-FLOOR ROLE. We are scaling fast. First person in the door who performs becomes the sales lead and builds the team. That means higher base, override commissions on your team's closes, and leadership title. But you earn it with results first.



HOW TO APPLY — ALL 4 REQUIRED:



1. Record yourself reading this script naturally (not robotic, not reading — deliver it like you're actually on the phone with a contractor):



"Hey Mike, your Vault report just came in. Over the last 7 days you missed 26 calls. Based on your average job value, that's about $94,000 a year in lost revenue. The good news — Vault caught every one of those and texted your customers back within 8 seconds. Want to keep it running? It's $147 a month."



2. Attach the recording



3. Tell me your outbound calling experience — where you worked, how many dials/day, what you sold, what your close rate was



4. Tell me when you can start



No recording = automatic rejection. I'm hiring this week.
