title: MCA Underwriter | Full-Time EST
description:
Hours: Mon–Fri, EST business hours overlap required





About the Role

We're a merchant cash advance company looking for a skilled underwriter to join our team. You'll be responsible for reviewing MCA applications, analyzing merchant financials, assessing risk, and making funding recommendations. This is a high-volume, fast-paced role where speed and accuracy both matter. You'll work directly with our sales and funding teams to move deals through the pipeline efficiently.



What You'll Do



Review and analyze incoming MCA applications and supporting documents

Examine 3–6 months of business bank statements to evaluate cash flow, daily balances, and revenue trends

Identify red flags such as NSFs, negative balances, stacking, and undisclosed positions

Calculate key metrics including average daily balance, monthly revenue, and deposit consistency

Determine funding amounts, hold-back percentages, and terms based on risk assessment

Issue approvals, declines, or request additional documentation with clear reasoning

Communicate underwriting decisions to the sales team with turnaround expectations

Maintain accurate records and notes in the CRM/underwriting platform

Meet daily volume targets (typically 15–25+ applications per day)

Stay current on MCA industry trends, compliance requirements, and risk factors





Requirements

Must-haves:



2+ years of hands-on MCA or alternative lending underwriting experience

Strong ability to read and interpret business bank statements quickly

Understanding of MCA-specific risk factors — stacking, split funding, seasonal businesses, etc.

Experience calculating daily balances, average monthly revenue, and position exposure

High attention to detail with the ability to spot inconsistencies and fraud indicators

Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-volume environment

Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

Available during US business hours (at least 9 AM–5 PM EST)

Strong written English for internal communication and deal notes



Nice-to-haves:



Experience with underwriting tools like DecisionLogic, Plaid, Ocrolus, or LendingFront

Familiarity with CRMs such as Salesforce, HubSpot, or industry-specific platforms

Background in other alternative lending products (revenue-based financing, business lines of credit, equipment financing)

Experience training or mentoring junior underwriters

Knowledge of UCC filings and lien searches





What We Offer



Stable, long-term full-time position — not a short-term project

Clear underwriting guidelines and support — you won't be guessing

Opportunity to grow into a senior or lead underwriter role

A team that values speed, good judgment, and reliability



How to Apply:


/t/vhQ7gK4vwsus?position=underwriter

title: Commercial Construction Project Estimator
description:
Join a commercial construction-related equipment manufacturer and play a key role in preparing accurate estimates and project bids to help them win more business.



This is not just a data entry role. You’ll be expected to think through the details, spot gaps, and make sure everything makes sense before pricing a project. You’ll have ownership of your work and contribute to building clear, accurate, and competitive proposals.



We’re looking for someone detail-oriented, reliable, and experienced in construction bidding who can manage multiple projects at the same time.



Responsibilities:

- Review project plans, specifications, drawings, and scope to understand requirements.

- Develop detailed cost estimates, proposals, and bid submissions.

- Interpret and annotate drawings based on project needs and constraints.

- Handle multiple bid requests simultaneously and meet deadlines.

- Work closely with project stakeholders, including Owners, Project Managers, Superintendents, and Subcontractors.

- Reach out to suppliers and subcontractors to gather pricing and ensure accurate proposals.

- Prepare and communicate complete proposals and estimates based on project requirements.

- Assist in pricing discussions and adjustments to stay competitive.



Requirements:

- 3+ years of experience with commercial construction bidding, whether as a supplier, general contractor, or subcontractor.

- 1+ year WFH experience supporting US-based clients.

- Experience with communicating and negotiating pricing is a major plus.

- Strong English communication skills, both written and verbal.

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable juggling multiple tasks.

- Comfortable managing a high volume of bids with accuracy and efficiency.

- Strong communication skills and ability to coordinate with multiple stakeholders.

- Organized, detail-oriented, and able to work independently.

- Must be available to work graveyard hours aligned with U.S. time zones.

- Reliable internet connection and dedicated work-from-home setup required.



Salary and Benefits:

- Base salary: 55,000 PHP per month

- 100% remote / work-from-home

- PTO and paid holidays



When applying, please send your resume link, explain why  you're a good fit. Also, copy and paste this Job ID: recN7dSXXziR82Mlq into your
 response, along with this message: “I’m excited to be applying to Job recN7dSXXziR82Mlq!” This will let us know what job you’re applying for as well as show us that you actually read this entire job description (attention to detail is everything). Thanks and hope to hear from you soon!

title: Work-from-Home Opportunity with Australian Criminal Law Firm – Long-Term Role | Weekend Shift (Sunday-Thursday)
description:
Work-from-Home Opportunity with Australian Criminal Law Firm – Long-Term Role | Weekend Shift (Sunday- Thursday)



We are a criminal law firm based in Australia, previously working with external call centres, but now building our dedicated in-house remote team. We currently have 5 valued tea
mbers based in the Philippines and are looking to add a Sixth for a long-term position.







Position Overview



This is a home-based, full-time role with the potential to earn up to PHP 100,000/month — starting at PHP 50,000 during training, with a raise to PHP 81,500 post-training, and bonuses up to PHP 10,000 based on commissions.







Schedule & Hours



-Permanent shift: From Sunday- Thursday



-Days off: Friday- Saturday



-11.5 hours per day (including a 45-minute lunch break)



-Working hours: 7:00 AM to approx. 6:30 PM ( There will be a rotation of roster so there are weeks where you only work from 6 AM to 2 PM Philippine time (aligned with Australian business hours and nightphones)



-Training schedule: Monday to Friday until fully trained



- Entitled to 15 vacation leaves







Your Duties Will Include:



-Answering incoming calls and handling client inquiries professionally



-Recording call details and booking appointments in our database



-Learning the basics of criminal law in Australia



-Providing frontline call management and transferring messages



-Managing live chat inquiries



- Collecting payment during after-hrs only



-Assisting with after-hours and weekend nightphones





What We're Looking For:



-Fluent English speaker with strong writing and spelling skills



-Reliable computer, high-speed internet, second monitor, and quality headphones (Bluetooth is preferred)



-Availability for full-time work only — no side jobs or agency representation



-A strong team player who is eager to learn, grow, and contribute long-term



-Willingness to work on Philippine holidays (with holiday pay already factored in)



-Rarely take leaves on a weekend.







Why Join Us?



-Work closely with a team of 35+ Australian lawyers and staff



-Join a growing and stable firm that values its remote team



-Receive thorough training and continuous support



-Enjoy long-term stability — our current PH team has been with us for 3+ years



-We pay the 13th month and offer performance-based bonuses







How to Apply:



-Please submit a voice and video introduction (e.g., via Vocaroo) sharing:



-A short introduction about yourself



-Your relevant experience



-Why you think you’d be a great fit for this role



-Provide your resume as well.



- Provide a typing test



-Only applicants who submit a voice or video introduction will be considered.







Do NOT apply if:



You’re an agency



You plan to take on another job



You don’t meet the equipment or availability requirements



We use real-time communication tools during working hours, and full commitment to this role is essential.







This is a great opportunity to join a respected and fast-growing Australian law firm ( Dribbin & Brown Criminal Lawyers). Before joining, we’re happy to connect you with our current Filipino staff so you can hear firsthand about the role and work environment.



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: FULLTIME Question Curator for Instagram (Remote| PH-based VA)
description:
We’re looking for a full-time Question Curator to join our team and help create high-performing question content for social media.



You’ll be working on content for our IG accounts under Flamingo Cards. This role is ideal for someone with strong judgment, great writing instincts, and an eye for what makes people want to answer (and share).



You do NOT need to be a “professional writer.” We care more about taste, structure, safety, and consistency. We’ll train you on our formats and what performs best.



What you’ll be doing:

*Curating and writing question sets for IG carousel posts

*Creating questions for themes like couples, friends, fun, deep, wholesome, etc.

*Turning a prompt/topic into 5 questions with intentional progression

*Making revisions based on feedback and performance data (link taps)



What we’re looking for:

*Strong English writing skills (simple, natural, not “AI sounding”)

*Excellent question judgment (knows what’s intriguing but still safe)

*Ability to follow formats and keep a consistent brand voice

*Taste for internet-friendly, scroll-stopping prompts (without being cringe)

*Comfortable writing lots of variations without repeating yourself

*Detail-oriented (spot duplicates, awkward phrasing, or overly similar prompts)

*Reliable internet connection

*Available for full-time work



Nice to have:

*Experience writing for social media marketing

*Familiarity with relationship / friendship / lifestyle content

*A small portfolio of writing samples (even a Google Doc is fine)



Salary, benefits, and growth:

*Starting salary is $400 per month (full-time)

*Performance-based raises

*We have tea
mbers who started at this level and now earn $1,000 per month after a couple of years

*Philippine holidays are observed

*13th month pay is provided

*This is a long-term role, not freelance or short-term



Why work with us:

*Stable, full-time position

*Supportive team that values improvement and consistency

*Training and guidance provided

*Clear growth path and raises for good performance

*Creative work you can be proud to ship (and see perform)

title: Appointment Setter
description:
Our lead generation system is strong - delivering 100+ warm leads per month - but we need someone with a track record to pick up the phone and get these prospects on the calendar.



This role is for you if:



- You have a verifiable history of hitting or exceeding targets.

- You know how to handle objections, qualify prospects, and secure commitment over the phone.



The Opportunity:



Set Your Schedule: Part-time or full-time options available (Between 20 - 40 hours per week).



Work Aussie Hours: Shifts scheduled between 8 AM and 6 PM (Melbourne Time).



Uncapped Commissions: The more appointments you book that show up, the more you earn!



Must-Haves:

- Excellent spoken English with a confident, professional phone voice.

- Proven track record in outbound appointment setting, B2B sales, customer service or solving customer queries.

jobs.interspeedia.com/jobs/17033.64053.59877.1

, Google Drive, etc.)

What We’re Looking For
